stories.title: Project Scaffold & Authentication
stories.acMarkdown:
### AC-1: Starter Template Initialized
**Given** no project exists yet
**When** the starter template is initialized via `npm create convex@latest xynapse -- -t nextjs-clerk-shadcn`
**Then** a working Next.js 16 + Convex + Clerk + shadcn/ui project is created
**And** day-one dependencies are installed: `convex-helpers`, `serwist`, `@serwist/next`, `vitest`, `convex-test`, `@axe-core/playwright`
**And** `next`, `convex`, and `@clerk/nextjs` are pinned to exact versions in package.json

### AC-2: Complete Convex Schema (7 collections)
**Given** the project is scaffolded
**When** the complete Convex schema is created in `convex/schema.ts`
**Then** all 7 collections are defined: `notes`, `categories`, `connections`, `mocs`, `auditLog`, `digests`, `userSettings`
**And** all indexes are defined including `by_user`, `by_user_status`, `by_user_category`, `by_parent`, `by_source`, `by_target`, `by_note`, `by_user_action`, `by_user_date`
**And** the vector index `by_embedding` is defined on `notes` with 1536 dimensions and `userId` filter field
**And** all field types match the architecture document exactly (e.g., `status` as union of `"pending" | "classified" | "failed" | "review"`)
**And** `categories.description: v.string()` is REQUIRED (not optional) — categories MUST include a semantic definition for classification prompts to inject
**And** `notes.entities: v.optional(v.object({ people: v.array(v.string()), projects: v.array(v.string()), concepts: v.array(v.string()) }))` is defined — populated during classification, enables entity-based linking in post-MVP phases
**And** `auditLog.details` supports optional fields: `promptVersion: v.optional(v.string())`, `wasEscalated: v.optional(v.boolean())`, `escalatedFrom: v.optional(v.id("auditLog"))` — these enable per-version accuracy analysis and escalation trail reconstruction

### AC-3: Schema Constraint Rationale
**Given** the schema supports the classification pipeline
**When** field constraints are reviewed
**Then** `categories.description` is required so the classification prompt has semantic definitions to inject (a category without a description cannot be used by the classifier)
**And** `notes.entities` is optional (absent for notes in `"pending"` or `"failed"` status; populated after successful classification)
**And** `auditLog.details` fields for `promptVersion`/`wasEscalated`/`escalatedFrom` are all optional to preserve backwards compatibility if the escalation or versioning features are disabled

### AC-4: Auth Wrappers
**Given** the schema is deployed
**When** `authenticatedQuery` and `authenticatedMutation` wrappers are created in `convex/lib/auth.ts`
**Then** they use `convex-helpers` `customQuery`/`customMutation` to extract `userId` from Clerk JWT via `ctx.auth.getUserIdentity()`
**And** they throw `ConvexError("Unauthenticated")` when no identity is present
**And** the wrappers attach `userId` to the context object for downstream use

### AC-5: Clerk Middleware
**Given** auth wrappers exist
**When** Clerk middleware is configured in `middleware.ts`
**Then** the `clerkMiddleware` protects all routes except `/sign-in(.*)` and `/sign-up(.*)`
**And** the matcher pattern excludes static files and `_next` routes: `["/((?!.*\\..*|_next).*)", "/", "/(api|trpc)(.*)"]`

### AC-6: OAuth Redirect Flow
**Given** Clerk middleware is configured
**When** a user visits any protected route without authentication
**Then** they are redirected to the Clerk sign-in page
**And** Google and GitHub OAuth providers are available as sign-in options (FR53)

### AC-7: Environment Variable Documentation
**Given** the project is scaffolded
**When** environment variable documentation is created
**Then** `.env.example` is committed listing all required variables with comments:
- `.env.local` vars: `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, `CLERK_SECRET_KEY`
- Convex env vars (via `npx convex env set`): `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY`, `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY`

**And** comments explain which vars go in `.env.local` vs Convex env

### AC-8: Vitest Configuration
**Given** the project is scaffolded
**When** `vitest.config.ts` is created
**Then** it configures vitest with `convex-test` and the `edge-runtime` environment
**And** test files use the `.test.ts` / `.test.tsx` naming convention (co-located with source files)

### AC-9: Serwist Wired
**Given** the project is scaffolded
**When** Serwist is configured in `next.config.ts`
**Then** the config uses Turbopack for dev and Webpack for production builds (Serwist compatibility)
**And** Serwist Service Worker generation is wired (plumbing only — no offline features yet)

### AC-10: Smoke Test Passes
**Given** all infrastructure is in place
**When** a smoke test is run in `convex/schema.test.ts`
**Then** the test validates the testing pattern works (vitest + convex-test + edge-runtime)
**And** the test passes successfully, confirming the dev environment is functional

### AC-11: GitHub Actions CI
**Given** the project is scaffolded
**When** the GitHub Actions CI workflow is committed at `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
**Then** the workflow runs on every push to `main` and every pull request
**And** the pipeline executes in this order: (1) `tsc --noEmit` type check, (2) `eslint` lint, (3) `vitest run` backend tests (edge-runtime environment), (4) `@axe-core/playwright` accessibility checks (wired in Story 7.4 — workflow step is scaffolded now but executes against real routes once they exist)
**And** the CI build FAILS if any step fails — main branch stays green
**And** Vercel auto-deploys on successful push to `main` (separate, via Vercel GitHub integration)
**And** Convex deploys via a pipeline step (`npx convex deploy`) using a deploy-key secret stored in GitHub Actions

---

stories.title: Capture Input & Thread Display
stories.acMarkdown:
**AC1 — Capture input renders (messaging model)**
Given the user is on the Capture tab, when the capture input renders, then:
1. An auto-expanding `<textarea>` is displayed at the bottom of the viewport (messaging model).
2. The input auto-focuses on mount, with the keyboard sliding up on mobile.
3. The input uses system font at 16px minimum (global rule from Story 1.2 — already enforced in `globals.css`).
4. The textarea auto-expands with content up to **40% of viewport height**, then scrolls internally.
5. No title field, category selector, or tag input is present — only the textarea and the send button.

**AC2 — Send behavior on mobile (< 768px)**
Given the input is rendered on mobile, then:
1. The send button (48×48px filled circle) is always visible to the right of the input.
2. Tapping the send button submits the capture.
3. Pressing **Enter inserts a newline** (multi-line brain dumps).
4. The send button is enabled only when input is non-empty (whitespace-only is empty).

**AC3 — Send behavior on desktop (≥ 768px)**
Given the input is rendered on desktop, then:
1. Pressing **Enter submits** the capture.
2. Pressing **Shift+Enter inserts a newline**.
3. The send button is also available for mouse/touch.

**AC4 — Submit flow (optimistic, non-blocking)**
Given the user types a thought and submits, then:
1. The input clears immediately.
2. A `createNote` mutation stores the capture in Convex with: `userId`, `content`, `status: "pending"`, `isAtomic: false`, `createdAt: Date.now()`.
3. A capture card appears in the thread above the input within **100ms** via Convex optimistic update (NFR2).
4. The card shows the full user text (up to 3 lines, then `line-clamp-3` with tap-to-expand).
5. The user can immediately start typing the next capture — the input is never blocked.

**AC5 — Pending card visual state**
Given a capture card is in `"pending"` status, when it renders, then:
1. No category chip is shown.
2. A subtle CSS **border pulse** animation plays (2000ms sinusoidal cycle) communicating "understanding your thought…".
3. The card has a 2px left-border in the neutral `--border` color.
4. The card uses `p-4` padding, `rounded-lg` with **square left corners** (`border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0`), no shadow.

**AC6 — Classification scheduling (defensive)**
Given the `createNote` mutation succeeds, when the note is stored, then:
1. A classification action is scheduled via `scheduler.runAfter(0, internal.classify.classifyNote, { noteId })`.
2. The mutation verifies **at least one category exists** before scheduling. If 0 categories exist, store with `status: "pending"` and **do not** schedule classification.

**AC7 — Burst capture integrity**
Given the user submits multiple captures rapidly, then:
1. Each card gets a unique ID tied to its Convex document.
2. Cards maintain chronological capture order — never reorder.
3. The input field is never blocked or displaced by incoming classifications.

**AC8 — Thread layout & auto-scroll**
Given the thread has multiple cards, when scrolled, then:
1. Recent captures are at the bottom, older captures above (messaging spatial model).
2. The thread auto-scrolls to the newest capture on submission.

**AC9 — Accessibility**
Given the capture input is evaluated for accessibility, then:
1. The textarea has `role="textbox"`, `aria-label="Capture a thought"`, `aria-multiline="true"`.
2. The send button has `aria-label="Send capture"`.
3. The pending card has `aria-busy="true"` and `aria-label="Understanding your thought"`.
4. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators.
5. Keyboard navigation: Tab moves between input and send button.

stories.title: AI Classification Pipeline

criteria.title: `classifyNote` builds the versioned prompt and calls OpenRouter
Zod validation at the AI boundary
OpenRouter API failure → retry then fail
Embedding chain (forward hook — Story 2.6, OUT OF SCOPE for 2.2)

stories.acMarkdown:
> Transcribed from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.2` (lines 712–785). The epics file is the **authoritative** contract (reconciled to the free-model policy in commit `9ebede8`). Where `architecture.md` differs, epics + the implemented `convex/schema.ts` win — see Dev Notes → "Source-of-truth & reconciliation".

**AC1 — `classifyNote` builds the versioned prompt and calls OpenRouter**
Given a note with `status: "pending"` exists and classification is scheduled, when the `classifyNote` internal action executes, then:
1. It reads the active prompt version via `getActivePromptVersion()`, which reads the `PROMPT_VERSION` Convex env var (default: `"v3-entity-extraction"`).
2. It builds the prompt via `buildClassificationPrompt(version, injections)` in `convex/lib/prompts.ts`.
3. Injections are assembled from: (a) the user's categories with their required `description` fields from the `categories` collection; (b) top-3 positive examples per category from `auditLog` (`action: "classification"`, `details.confidence > 0.85`, excluding notes that were subsequently corrected); (c) the last 20 corrections from `auditLog` (`userId`, `action: "correction"`, ordered by `createdAt` ascending so the newest appears **last** — LLMs weight later examples more heavily); (d) the capture text.
4. The built prompt contains five sections in order: **Categories** (name + id + description per category), **Examples of Confident Classifications** (top-3 per category, grouped by category header), **Recent Corrections** (chronological, oldest-first), **Capture to Classify** (triple-quoted), **Instructions** (classify + extract entities + JSON output schema).
5. The action calls OpenRouter with the model from the `CLASSIFICATION_MODEL` env var (default `qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free`), using OpenRouter `structured_outputs`, passing the built `system` and `user` prompts.
6. The expected response format is `{ isAtomic: boolean, notes: [{ content, categoryId, confidence, reasoning, entities: { people, projects, concepts } }] }`, and each `entities` sub-field is a `string[]` (may be empty when no entities of that type are present).

**AC2 — Zod validation at the AI boundary**
Given the LLM returns a response, when the response is parsed, then:
1. It is validated using Zod `safeParse` with the `ClassificationResponseSchema`.
2. The schema validates: `isAtomic` (boolean); `notes` array (min 1 entry); each entry has `content` (string, min 1), `categoryId` (string), `confidence` (number 0–1), `reasoning` (string), and an `entities` object containing `people` (string[]), `projects` (string[]), `concepts` (string[]).
3. If validation fails, the action returns `{ success: false, error: result.error.message, retryable: false }` (a malformed response is **not** retryable — retrying the same prompt will not fix bad JSON).

**AC3 — Store a single-idea classification**
Given a valid single-idea classification response (`isAtomic: true`, exactly 1 note), when the classification is stored via the `storeClassification` internal mutation, then:
1. The note is updated with `categoryId`, `confidence`, and `status: "classified"` (or `"review"` if confidence is below the user's threshold).
2. The note's `entities` field is populated from the LLM response (`entities: { people, projects, concepts }`).
3. The note's `isAtomic` is set to `true` (single-idea confirmed).
4. An `auditLog` entry is created with `userId`, `noteId`, `action: "classification"`, `details: { newCategory, confidence, modelUsed: <CLASSIFICATION_MODEL>, reasoning, promptVersion }`, `createdAt` (cross-cut: implements FR43 audit logging).
5. `promptVersion` in `details` is the string value of the active version used for this classification call — this per-capture tagging enables the Week 6 per-version accuracy comparison query.

> Multi-idea responses (`isAtomic: false` or `notes.length > 1`) are **Story 2.5's** scope (atomic split + linking). For 2.2, see Dev Notes → "Scope boundary: single-idea only" for the required graceful fallback so a multi-idea capture is never left stuck in `pending`.

**AC4 — Low-confidence escalation routing (FR9)**
Given a classification confidence below the user's `confidenceThreshold` (default `0.60`), when the escalation routing is evaluated in `classify.ts`, then the flow executes:
1. (step 1) Write a first `auditLog` entry for the classification-model attempt with `details.wasEscalated: true`.
2. (step 2) Check the `ESCALATION_ENABLED` Convex env var.
3. (step 3) If `ESCALATION_ENABLED === true`: call the escalation model `nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free` via OpenRouter using the **same** prompt template; write a second `auditLog` entry with `details.escalatedFrom: <first entry id>`, `modelUsed: "nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free"`, and `promptVersion`; then use the escalation model's result — status `"classified"` if its confidence ≥ 0.60, `"review"` otherwise.
4. (step 4) If `ESCALATION_ENABLED === false` (MVP default): skip the escalation call and route directly to `status: "review"` based on the classification model's low confidence (FR9).
5. When both models return low confidence (< 0.60), the note goes to `status: "review"` — **not** "higher confidence of the two" (honest uncertainty preserves the trust architecture over fabricated confidence).
6. The note document stores only the **final** result (the escalation model's if escalated); the full escalation trail lives in `auditLog` via the two linked entries.
7. The `ESCALATION_ENABLED` feature flag is a Convex env var, switchable via `npx convex env set ESCALATION_ENABLED=true` after the Week 6 gate review. **MVP ships with the flag `false` — the escalation model call is defined in code but not invoked.**
8. Escalation cost is bounded: only ~15–20% of captures fall below threshold, so the escalation model is called only for those (not every classification).

**AC5 — OpenRouter API failure → retry then fail**
Given the OpenRouter API call fails (network error, timeout, 5xx), when the action handles the failure, then:
1. It returns `{ success: false, error, retryable: true }`.
2. A retry is scheduled with exponential backoff: 1min, 5min, 30min (`RETRY_DELAYS = [60_000, 300_000, 1_800_000]`, max 3 retries).
3. After 3 failed retries, the note status is set to `"failed"` via the `markFailed` internal mutation.
4. The error is logged via `console.error` **without exposing user note content** (NFR41).

**AC6 — Daily rate-limit gate (NFR40, NFR19)**
Given the rate-limit check runs before each API call, when the daily classification count is checked via the atomic `incrementAndCheck` mutation, then:
1. If the count exceeds 40, the classification is **queued for the next day** rather than rejected (NFR40).
2. The capture remains with `status: "pending"` — the capture is never lost (NFR19).

**AC7 — Embedding chain (forward hook — Story 2.6, OUT OF SCOPE for 2.2)**
Given the classification action completes successfully, when the embedding pipeline exists (Story 2.6), then `scheduler.runAfter(0, internal.embed.generateEmbedding, { noteId })` is called to chain embedding, and embedding failure does not affect classification display (decoupled via scheduler).
> `convex/embed.ts` does not exist yet (Story 2.6). Wiring this call now would break `tsc`. For 2.2, leave a clearly-labeled placeholder comment at the success point and do **not** create the chain. See Dev Notes → "Forward dependency: embedding chain".

**AC8 — Zod schema companion tests**
Given companion tests for the Zod validation schema exist, when the tests run, then they verify: valid input acceptance, missing-field rejection, wrong-type rejection, confidence boundary values (0.0, 1.0, negative, > 1), and empty-`notes`-array rejection. (Enforcement rule 12: every Zod schema must have companion tests.)

**AC9 — Prompt infrastructure (`convex/lib/prompts.ts`)**
Given this is the first AI pipeline action using a versioned prompt, when the prompt infrastructure is created, then `convex/lib/prompts.ts` exports:
1. A `PromptVersion` type union — at minimum `"v3-entity-extraction"`, with historical labels (`"v1-baseline"`, `"v2-positive-fewshot"`) permitted.
2. A `PROMPTS` record of `{ system: string; userTemplate: string }` per version — `v3-entity-extraction` contains the 5-section user template (Categories / Examples of Confident Classifications / Recent Corrections / Capture to Classify / Instructions) and a system prompt describing the classifier role and strict-JSON output requirement.
3. `getActivePromptVersion(): PromptVersion` — reads `process.env.PROMPT_VERSION`, falls back to `"v3-entity-extraction"`.
4. `buildClassificationPrompt(version, injections): { system: string; user: string }` — string interpolation for `{user_name}`, `{category_block}`, `{positive_examples_block}`, `{corrections_block}`, `{capture_text}`.
5. The positive-examples query reads `auditLog` filtered by `action: "classification"` with `details.confidence > 0.85`, cross-referenced against correction entries for the same `noteId` to exclude subsequently-corrected captures, then groups by `categoryId` and takes the top 3 highest-confidence entries per category.
6. The `PROMPT_VERSION` Convex env var can be switched at runtime via `npx convex env set PROMPT_VERSION=<version>` with no code deploy (adding a brand-new version still requires adding its entry to the `PROMPTS` record in a subsequent deploy).

**AC10 — Shared AI-pipeline utilities (reusable by 2.6 / 4.1 / 4.3)**
Given this is the first AI pipeline action in the project, when shared utilities are created, then:
1. `convex/lib/validation.ts` contains `ClassificationResponseSchema` (the first Zod schema — will grow with later AI stories, then split per-domain at ~150 lines).
2. `convex/lib/retry.ts` contains the exponential-backoff retry pattern (1min/5min/30min, max 3 retries) — a phase override from architecture's `[1B]` marking; needed here in `[1A]` because 2.2 is the first external-API integration.
3. `convex/lib/rateLimits.ts` contains the atomic `incrementAndCheck` mutation (configurable per-type daily cap: **40 classifications/day** to stay under OpenRouter's free 50/day `:free` ceiling, **100 embeddings/day**) — also a `[1B]→[1A]` phase override.
4. These utilities (including `convex/lib/prompts.ts`) are reusable by subsequent stories (embedding in 2.6, clustering in 4.1, digest synthesis in 4.3).

stories.title: Classification Display & Confidence Feedback
stories.acMarkdown:
> Transcribed from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.3` (lines 788–842). The epics file is the **authoritative** contract. Where `architecture.md` or `ux-design-specification.md` differ, epics + the implemented `convex/schema.ts` + existing code win — see Dev Notes → "Source-of-truth & reconciliation".

**AC1 — Pending → classified transition (reactive)**
Given a note transitions from `"pending"` to `"classified"` in Convex, when the reactive subscription on the capture card detects the change, then:
1. A `ConfidenceBadge` (category chip) animates onto the card via a 300ms ease-out slide-in (`@keyframes slideIn` per spec — reuse the existing `slideUp` 300ms ease-out keyframe; see Dev Notes → "Animations").
2. The border-pulse animation stops (the card is no longer `pending`).
3. The 2px left-border updates from neutral `--border` to the category's HSL color (treatment follows the confidence variant — see AC2–AC4).
4. The timestamp is displayed in `text-sm text-muted-foreground`, aligned right on the card header row.

**AC2 — High confidence (> 85%)**
Given the classification has high confidence (`confidence > 0.85`), when the `ConfidenceBadge` renders, then:
1. The chip has a **solid** category-color fill with dark text.
2. **No** microcopy is displayed below the card.
3. The left-border uses the **full solid** category color.

**AC3 — Medium confidence (60–85%)**
Given the classification has medium confidence (`0.60 ≤ confidence ≤ 0.85`), when the `ConfidenceBadge` renders, then:
1. The chip uses category color at **40% opacity (light) / 30% (dark)** with category-colored text and a subtle "?" icon.
2. Microcopy appears below the card: **"I'm not sure about this one — does this look right?"**
3. The left-border uses the muted category color matching the chip opacity.

**AC4 — Low confidence (< 60%, status `"review"`)**
Given the classification has low confidence (`confidence < 0.60`) with `status: "review"`, when the `ConfidenceBadge` renders, then:
1. The chip uses category color at **20% opacity (light) / 15% (dark)** with a **dashed** border.
2. Microcopy appears below the card: **"This one's tricky — where does it belong?"**
3. The left-border uses a **dashed** style matching the chip.

**AC5 — Failed status**
Given a note has `status: "failed"`, when the card renders, then: no chip, **muted card opacity**, microcopy: **"Saved — I'll classify this when I reconnect"**.

**AC6 — No semantic content**
Given a capture has no semantic content (e.g. `"test"`, `"asdf"`, `"."`) and the classification cannot determine a category, when the card renders, then: no chip, microcopy: **"I'll keep this safe — you can categorize it later if you'd like"**.
> The 2.2 backend as shipped does not emit a "no-category" outcome (it always assigns a real `categoryId` or routes to `failed`). 2.3 implements the **display** of this state, keyed on a `classified`/`review` note that has **no `categoryId`** — see Dev Notes → "The no-semantic-content state (data representation)". This is defensive/forward-compatible rendering; emitting the state from the classifier is out of scope.

**AC7 — Burst capture (staggered animation)**
Given multiple classifications arrive simultaneously, when 2+ cards receive classification within the same render cycle, then chip animations **stagger with a 200ms delay** between each.

**AC8 — Needs Review filter (FR14)**
Given the user views items pending review, when they access the **"Needs Review"** filter, then all notes with `status: "review"` are shown with the medium/low confidence treatment inviting correction.

**AC9 — Accessibility**
Given classification display is evaluated for accessibility, when card state transitions are inspected, then:
1. Classification arrival triggers an `aria-live="polite"` announcement: **"Classified as [category], [confidence level] confidence"**.
2. The `ConfidenceBadge` has `role="button"`, `aria-label="Category: [name], [confidence level] confidence. Tap to change"`.
3. Microcopy is linked to the badge via `aria-describedby`.
4. All animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (instant appear — no slide/pulse).

stories.title: One-Tap Correction & Learning Signals
stories.acMarkdown:
> Transcribed from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.4` (lines 846–895). The epics file is the **authoritative** contract. Where `architecture.md` or `ux-design-specification.md` differ, epics + the implemented `convex/schema.ts` + existing code win — see Dev Notes → "Source-of-truth & reconciliation".

**AC1 — Open the correction dropdown**
Given a classified capture card is displayed, when the user taps the category chip (`ConfidenceBadge`), then:
1. A Dropdown Menu opens showing **all** user-defined categories ordered by AI confidence (highest first — see Dev Notes → "Dropdown ordering" for the reconciled, buildable ordering).
2. Each item is colored with its HSL-generated category color.
3. The currently assigned category is visually indicated.

**AC2 — Optimistic chip update**
Given the correction dropdown is open, when the user taps a different category, then:
1. The chip updates **instantly** to the new category via optimistic update (local state before mutation).
2. The left-border color updates to match.
3. A `correctClassification` public mutation fires in the background.

**AC3 — Persist correction + learning signal**
Given the `correctClassification` mutation executes, when the correction is stored, then:
1. The note's `categoryId` is updated and `status` set to `"classified"` (if it was `"review"`).
2. An `auditLog` entry is created with: `userId`, `noteId`, `action: "correction"`, `details: { previousCategory, newCategory, confidence: originalConfidence }`, `createdAt` (cross-cut: FR43, FR46).
   - `previousCategory` / `newCategory` are **category `Id`s** (`v.id("categories")`), `confidence` is the note's **original** confidence read **before** the patch. This exact shape is already consumed by the classifier's few-shot loop — see Dev Notes → "The learning loop is already wired".

**AC4 — Confirmation toast (normal vs high-confidence override) (FR13)**
Given the correction is saved successfully, when the toast feedback appears, then:
1. For **normal** corrections (original confidence ≤ 85%): **"Got it — I'll remember that for next time."**
2. For **high-confidence overrides** (original confidence > 85%): a distinct acknowledgment toast (FR13).
3. The high-confidence toast uses a **TEMPLATE (not an LLM call)** to avoid added latency/cost on every correction: **``Got it — I'll weight `[previousCategoryName]` less next time when context points to `[newCategoryName]`.``** where the bracketed names are inserted from the category documents.
4. The toast is **non-blocking, auto-dismisses after 3 seconds, never stacks** (a new toast replaces the existing one).

**AC5 — Failure revert**
Given the correction mutation fails, when the optimistic update reverts, then:
1. The chip and left-border revert to the original category.
2. A toast appears: **"Couldn't save — try again"**.

**AC6 — Corrections feed future classification (FR12)**
Given corrections accumulate over time, when the next classification runs (Story 2.2), then the last 20 corrections are included as few-shot examples, chronological with the most recent last (FR12).
> This is **already implemented** in `convex/classify.ts` → `getClassificationContext` (it reads `auditLog` `action:"correction"` rows). 2.4 satisfies this AC by **writing the correctly-shaped rows** (AC3) and **proving the loop** with an integration test — see Dev Notes + Task 3.

**AC7 — Accessibility**
Given the correction dropdown is evaluated for accessibility, when the component is inspected, then:
1. The dropdown uses `role="menu"` with `role="menuitem"` per category.
2. ↑↓ keys navigate, Enter selects, Escape dismisses.
3. Focus is trapped inside while open.
4. The chip has `aria-haspopup="menu"` and an `aria-expanded` state.
5. The toast uses `role="status"` for screen-reader announcement.
6. All items have visible focus indicators.

stories.title: Atomic Note Extraction & Linking

criteria.title: The classifier may return a multi-idea response
Backend: store the split (the core data write)
Frontend: split cards render with a Captured-Together link
Captured-Together link expands to show origin + merge action

stories.acMarkdown:
> Transcribed from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.5` (lines 898–943). The epics file is the **authoritative** contract. Where `architecture.md` or `ux-design-specification.md` differ, epics + the implemented `convex/schema.ts` + existing code win — see Dev Notes → "Source-of-truth & reconciliation".

**AC1 — The classifier may return a multi-idea response**
Given a user submits a capture containing multiple distinct ideas, when the classification action (Story 2.2) processes it, then the LLM may return `isAtomic: false` with multiple entries in the `notes` array. *(This is already what the 2.2 Zod schema accepts and the prompt asks for — 2.5 adds the handling.)*

**AC2 — Backend: store the split (the core data write)**
Given an atomic split is detected (**2+ notes** in the response), when the split is processed, then:
1. The original note (the capture) is **kept with its full text** and `isAtomic: false` — it becomes the hidden *parent/container* (preserves the original for the link preview + merge-back).
2. A **child note** is created for each extracted idea with: `content`, `categoryId`, `confidence`, `status` (per the same threshold rule as 2.2 — `"classified"` if `confidence ≥ confidenceThreshold` else `"review"`), `parentCaptureId` (the original note's `_id`), `capturedTogetherGroup` (a single shared UUID for all siblings), `isAtomic: true`, `entities`, `createdAt`.
3. A `connections` document is created for **each sibling pair** with `source: "captured_together"`, `userId`, `sourceNoteId`, `targetNoteId`, `similarity: 1.0`, `createdAt` (N children → `C(N,2)` connections; N=2 → 1, N=3 → 3).
4. An `auditLog` entry (`action: "classification"`) is created for **each child note's** classification (`details: { newCategory, confidence, modelUsed, reasoning, promptVersion }`).

**AC3 — Frontend: split cards render with a Captured-Together link**
Given an atomic split has been processed, when the capture thread renders the result, then:
1. **Individual cards appear for each extracted (child) note** with their own confidence chip and left-border color (reuse the existing `ClassificationCard`).
2. A **CapturedTogetherLink** appears between sibling cards: a 2px vertical connecting line + 🔗 icon + the text "Captured together" in `text-xs muted-foreground`.
3. The parent/container note does **not** render as its own card.

**AC4 — Captured-Together link expands to show origin + merge action**
Given the CapturedTogetherLink is displayed, when the user taps it, then:
1. An expanded view shows: **"Captured together on [date] from: '[first 50 chars of original]...'"**.
2. A **"Merge back into one note"** action is available.

**AC5 — Merge back into one note (FR4)**
Given the user taps "Merge back into one note", when the merge executes, then:
1. The child notes are **deleted**.
2. The parent note is **restored** with `status: "pending"` (which **re-triggers classification**).
3. The `connections` entries with `source: "captured_together"` for these notes are **deleted**.
4. A toast appears: **"Got it — I'll keep these together"**.

**AC6 — Many-split (3+) collapse**
Given a capture splits into **3+ notes**, when the thread renders, then:
1. The **first 2 cards display fully**; the remaining appear as an **"and N more notes"** expandable group.
2. Tapping the group reveals all split cards with their classifications and links.

**AC7 — Accessibility**
Given atomic-split components are evaluated for accessibility, when inspected, then:
1. The CapturedTogetherLink has `role="button"`, `aria-label="These notes were captured together. Tap to see original or merge"`.
2. The merge button has `aria-label="Merge back into one note"`.
3. Splits are announced via `aria-live="polite"`: **"Captured. Split into [N] notes: [category 1], [category 2]"**.
4. All elements are keyboard-navigable; the sibling cards are wrapped in `role="group"` with `aria-label="Captures from same thought"`.

stories.title: Embedding Generation Pipeline
stories.acMarkdown:
> Transcribed from `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 2.6` (lines 947–983). The epics file is the **authoritative** contract. Where `architecture.md` differs, epics + the implemented `convex/schema.ts` + existing code win — see Dev Notes → "Source-of-truth & reconciliation".

**AC1 — Generate the embedding (chained after classification)**
Given a note has been **successfully classified** (Story 2.2 chains this), when the `generateEmbedding` internal action fires via `scheduler.runAfter(0, ...)`, then:
1. It calls the configured **free embedding provider — Google `gemini-embedding-001`, finalized this story** — at **1536-dim output** so the existing `notes.by_embedding` vector index is unchanged, **directly** (not via OpenRouter, which has no embedding models).
2. The input is the note's **`content`** field.
3. The response returns a **1536-dimensional vector**.

**AC2 — Store the vector + audit**
Given the embedding is generated successfully, when the vector is stored, then:
1. The note's **`embedding`** field is updated with the float64 array.
2. The vector is indexed by **`by_embedding`** with the `userId` filter (automatic — it is the schema's `vectorIndex`; storing the field indexes it).
3. An **`auditLog`** entry is created with: `userId`, `noteId`, `action: "embedding"`, `details: { modelUsed: <configured embedding model id, e.g. "gemini-embedding-001"> }`, `createdAt`.

**AC3 — Failure never affects classification/capture (NFR41)**
Given the embedding API call fails, when the failure is handled, then:
1. A retry is scheduled with **exponential backoff (1min, 5min, 30min, max 3)** — reuse `convex/lib/retry.ts`.
2. The note **remains classified and visible** — embedding failure **never** changes the note's `status` (no `markFailed`-style write touches `notes.status` in this pipeline).
3. The error is logged via **`console.error` without exposing note `content`** (NFR41) — log the note id only.

**AC4 — Rate limit: queue, never reject (NFR40)**
Given the embedding rate limit is checked, when the daily count exceeds **100**, then the embedding is **queued for the next day** (via `scheduler.runAfter(msUntilNextUtcMidnight, ...)`) rather than rejected. Uses the existing atomic `internal.lib.rateLimits.incrementAndCheck` with `type: "embedding"` (the 100/day cap already lives in `convex/lib/rateLimits.ts`).

**AC5 — Timing (NFR5)**
Given embedding generation timing is measured, when the action completes, then it finishes **within 1 second per note** (async, non-blocking). This is provider latency for a single `embedContent` call; the pipeline adds no blocking work. Set a request timeout to abort a hung call (see Dev Notes).

**AC6 — Embedding response validation (companion tests)**
Given companion tests for embedding response validation exist, when tests run, then they verify: **valid 1536-dim array acceptance**, **wrong-dimension rejection**, **non-numeric rejection**, and **empty/malformed response handling**.

stories.title: Note Browsing & Category Filtering
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story 3.1` (BDD groups AC1–AC8). The dev MUST satisfy every clause.

**AC1 — Notes list renders (Browse tab):**
1. All of the user's captured notes are displayed (filtered by `userId` via `authenticatedQuery`).
2. Notes are sorted by `createdAt` **descending** (newest first) — opposite of the capture thread.
3. Each note renders as a compact card showing: category chip (ConfidenceBadge), capture text (truncated `line-clamp-2`), relative timestamp, and a 2px left-border accent in the category color.
4. Notes with `status: "pending"` or `"failed"` are included, with their respective visual treatments from Story 2.3.

**AC2 — Loading skeletons:**
1. While data has not yet arrived from Convex, shaped skeleton cards are displayed matching the card layout (chip placeholder + text lines).
2. Skeletons use a slow 2s pulse animation.
3. Skeletons appear only after a **200ms delay** (prevents flash for fast loads).

**AC3 — Desktop category sidebar (≥ 768px):**
1. The contextual sidebar shows the user's categories as a list with **note counts per category**.
2. Each category item is colored with its HSL color.
3. Tapping a category filters the notes list to only that category.

**AC4 — Mobile category filter (< 768px):**
1. A category filter bar or dropdown is available above the notes list.
2. Selecting a category filters the list; selecting "All" removes the filter.

**AC5 — Category-empty state:**
1. When a category filter is active and no notes exist in that category, the empty state shows: `"No captures in [Category] yet."`
2. No CTA button is shown.

**AC6 — Zero-captures empty state:**
1. When the user has zero captures total, the Browse tab shows: `"Your captures will appear here."`
2. No guilt-inducing messaging (design-for-restart principle).

**AC7 — Accessibility:**
1. The list uses `role="feed"` or `role="list"` with `aria-label="Your captures"`.
2. Each note card uses `role="article"` with an `aria-label` including category and truncated text.
3. Category filter controls are keyboard-navigable.
4. All elements have visible focus indicators.

stories.title: Note Detail View & Deletion
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story 3.2` (BDD groups AC1–AC7). The dev MUST satisfy every clause.

**AC1 — Opening the detail view (responsive):**
1. The user taps a note card (Browse tab or capture thread) → the note detail opens.
2. On mobile (< 768px): a bottom Sheet slides up (half-screen default, draggable/expandable toward full-screen).
3. On desktop (≥ 768px): the right detail panel shows the note detail (no overlay needed).

**AC2 — Detail content:**
1. Full capture text, **no truncation** (`whitespace-pre-wrap`).
2. Category chip with the confidence variant (reuse `ConfidenceBadge`).
3. Timestamp formatted via `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with the user's locale/timezone (absolute, not the relative "3h").
4. Correction history — list of `auditLog` rows for this note with `action: "correction"`.
5. The AI's classification reasoning — from the note's `auditLog` `action: "classification"` row, `details.reasoning`.
6. For atomic child notes (`parentCaptureId` present): a "Part of a capture" link showing the original capture text and sibling notes.
7. If the note has an `embedding`: a "Related Notes" section **placeholder** (populated in Story 3.3).

**AC3 — Correction history display:**
1. Each correction shows: previous category → new category, the original confidence, and a timestamp.
2. Entries are ordered **chronologically (oldest first)** to show the learning progression.

**AC4 — Delete action + confirmation:**
1. The delete action is reachable via swipe-to-reveal (mobile), right-click menu (desktop), or a button in the detail view.
2. A confirmation dialog appears: `"Delete this capture? This can't be undone."`
3. The confirm button uses `--destructive` color and is labeled `"Delete"` (not generic "OK").
4. Cancel is on the **left**, Delete on the **right**.

**AC5 — Confirmed deletion:**
1. The `deleteNote` mutation deletes the note document from the `notes` collection.
2. Associated `connections` entries (where `sourceNoteId` **or** `targetNoteId` matches) are deleted.
3. Associated `auditLog` entries are **NOT** deleted (audit history preserved).
4. If the note was a child of an atomic split, the captured-together link updates to reflect the removal.
5. A toast appears: `"Capture deleted"` with a **5-second undo** option.
6. Tapping "Undo" within 5 seconds restores the note.

**AC6 — Mobile Sheet overlay behavior:**
1. The Sheet can be dismissed by: swiping down, tapping the scrim (dimmed background), or tapping a close button.
2. Maximum 1 Sheet open at a time (opening a new one replaces the current).
3. Focus is trapped inside the Sheet and Escape dismisses it.
4. The Sheet pushes up when the keyboard appears (for any input within the Sheet). *(N/A for this story — the detail Sheet has no text input; see Dev Notes.)*

**AC7 — Accessibility:**
1. Mobile Sheet and desktop panel have proper focus management (focus trapped in Sheet, auto-focus on open, return focus on close).
2. Semantic heading structure: `<h2>` for the note, `<h3>` for sections.
3. The correction history uses proper `<table>` semantics with accessible headers.
4. The delete confirmation dialog has `role="alertdialog"` with `aria-describedby` pointing to the description text.
5. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators and are keyboard-navigable.

stories.title: Related Notes Panel

criteria.title: Section appears + vector search (note has an embedding):
"Still forming" (recent note, embedding not ready yet):

stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim intent from `epics.md#Story 3.3` (BDD groups AC1–AC6). The dev MUST satisfy every clause.

**AC1 — Section appears + vector search (note has an embedding):**
1. When viewing a note detail (Story 3.2) **and** the note has a value in its `embedding` field → a "Related Notes" section appears in the detail view.
2. A `getRelatedNotes` **public action** is called with the `noteId`.
3. The action performs a Convex **vector search** on the `by_embedding` index filtered by `userId`, returning the **top 5** most similar notes, **excluding the current note**.

**AC2 — Results render:**
1. Up to 5 related-note cards are displayed, each showing: category chip, capture text (truncated `line-clamp-2`), relative timestamp, and a **similarity indicator** (visual only — **no numeric score** in the main UI).
2. Results include notes from **ANY category** and **ANY time period** — cross-category and cross-time connections are surfaced (FR17).
3. Tapping a related note opens **its** detail view (mobile: replaces the current Sheet; desktop: navigates the detail panel).

**AC3 — "Still forming" (recent note, embedding not ready yet):**
1. When the note was recently captured and its embedding is still being generated → the section shows: `"Connections are still forming..."` in `text-xs muted-foreground`.
2. The section **automatically refreshes** when the embedding becomes available (no manual reload required).

**AC4 — Empty (no similar notes found):**
1. When the vector search returns no results → the section shows: `"No connections yet — keep capturing and I'll find patterns"`.

**AC5 — Performance:**
1. The search returns within **500ms** for knowledge bases up to 100K notes (NFR6). (Reusing the stored embedding means **no embedding-API round-trip** here — only the vector search + a bounded hydrate, so this budget is comfortable.)

**AC6 — Accessibility:**
1. The section has `role="region"` with `aria-label="Related notes"`.
2. Each related-note card is focusable and activatable via keyboard.
3. The `"Connections are still forming..."` message uses `aria-live="polite"` to announce when connections appear.
4. All cards have visible focus indicators.

stories.title: Semantic Search
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim intent from `epics.md#Story 3.4` (BDD groups AC1–AC10). The dev MUST satisfy every clause. Where the epics and the UX spec both speak, the **exact copy strings below are authoritative** (cross-checked against `ux-design-specification.md`).

**AC1 — Mobile trigger (pull-down):**
1. On the Capture tab or Browse tab, on **mobile** (`< 768px`), pulling down from the **top of the capture thread** opens a **bottom Sheet** with a search input at the top.
2. The input shows a search icon and the placeholder `"Search your captures..."`.

**AC2 — Desktop trigger (⌘K / Ctrl+K):**
1. On **desktop** (`≥ 768px`), pressing **⌘K** (Mac) or **Ctrl+K** (Windows) opens a **floating, centered search panel** overlaying the main content.
2. The input **auto-focuses** with placeholder `"Search your captures..."`.

**AC3 — Debounce + cancel-on-type:**
1. Typing a natural-language query debounces **300ms** before triggering a search.
2. Each new keystroke (after debounce) **aborts/supersedes the previous in-flight search** (cancel-on-type) — only the latest query's results may render.
3. A **subtle loading indicator** appears while a search is in progress.

**AC4 — `searchNotes` action (query embedding + vector search):**
1. The debounced query calls a **public `searchNotes` action**.
2. It generates a **query embedding** via the same free provider finalized in Story 2.6 — Google `gemini-embedding-001` at **1536-dim** output (so the existing `by_embedding` index is unchanged), using task type **`RETRIEVAL_QUERY`** (NOT `RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT`).
3. It performs a Convex **vector search** on `by_embedding` filtered by `userId`.
4. Returns results **ranked by semantic similarity (not recency)**.

**AC5 — Results render:**
1. Each result is a card: capture text (truncated) + **category chip** + **relative date**.
2. Results are ordered by **relevance (similarity score)**, not chronologically.
3. Only results from the **most recent** query are displayed (stale results discarded — see AC3.2).
4. Tapping a result opens the note detail (Sheet on mobile, detail panel on desktop).

**AC6 — Empty results state:**
1. When the search returns no matches → `"No captures match that — try different words or a broader description"`.

**AC7 — Empty input state:**
1. When the panel is open with **no query typed** → `"Search across all your captures — try describing what you're looking for"`.

**AC8 — Dismiss:**
1. Pressing **Escape** closes the panel and returns focus to the previous context.

**AC9 — Performance (NFR6):**
1. Results return within **500ms** (embedding generation + vector search combined).

**AC10 — Accessibility:**
1. The search input has `role="searchbox"` with `aria-label="Search your captures"`.
2. Results use `role="listbox"` with `aria-label="Search results"`.
3. Each result has `role="option"` and is keyboard-selectable (**↑/↓** to navigate, **Enter** to open).
4. The search panel **traps focus** and **Escape** dismisses (Radix Sheet/Dialog supplies both).
5. The loading state is communicated via **`aria-busy="true"`** on the results container.

> **Search failure (UX spec line 1819, treat as binding):** when the embedding/search call **fails** (provider error, network), the results area shows the error copy `"Search isn't working right now — try again in a moment"` — this is **distinct from AC6** ("no matches"). A failure MUST NOT render as "no matches."

---

stories.title: Re-Embedding Batch Utility
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim intent from `epics.md#Story 3.5` (BDD groups AC1–AC5). The dev MUST satisfy every clause. This is a **backend-only** story — there is no UI AC (the Settings-hub trigger UI is Epic 6, Story 6.4). The deliverable is the `reEmbedAllNotes` action + its internal machinery and tests.

**AC1 — Iterate & re-embed (the `reEmbedAllNotes` action):**
1. When the `reEmbedAllNotes` action executes, it iterates **all** of the caller's notes using **paginated reads** (NOT `.collect()` / not loading every note at once).
2. For each note **with content** (`content.trim() !== ""`), it generates a **new embedding** via the **configured** embedding model (the stored-document task type `RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT`, 1536-dim).
3. The new embedding **replaces** the existing one on the note document (`notes.embedding`).
4. An `auditLog` entry with `action: "embedding"` is created for **each** re-embedded note (`details.modelUsed` = the configured model).

**AC2 — Rate limiting, next-day spill, idempotency:**
1. Rate limiting is respected: the **same per-user 100 embeddings/day** gate the live pipeline uses (`internal.lib.rateLimits.incrementAndCheck`, `type: "embedding"`), default **100/day**, **configurable**.
2. If the daily limit is reached mid-batch, the **remaining** notes are scheduled for the next day via `scheduler.runAfter()` (queue, never reject — NFR40), resuming where it left off.
3. The process is **idempotent / safe to retry**: re-running never corrupts data, never throws to the caller, and converges to *every note carrying a current-model embedding*. (Idempotency is about the embedding end-state — audit rows accumulate one-per-embedding-event by design, see Dev Notes → "Idempotency vs. audit rows".)

**AC3 — Per-note error isolation:**
1. When the re-embedding process hits an error on a **single** note, the error is **logged** and the process **continues with the next note** (NO abort on a single failure).
2. Failed notes are **tracked for later retry** (a re-run of the utility re-attempts them — see Dev Notes → "Failed-note tracking").

**AC4 — Configurable model (NFR37):**
1. The embedding model identifier can be changed in **configuration** (`EMBEDDING_MODEL` env var) **without code changes**.
2. The utility uses the **configured** model (`process.env.EMBEDDING_MODEL ?? DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL`), **never a hardcoded model name**. The audit row's `details.modelUsed` records the model actually used.

**AC5 — Personal-scale performance & post-completion correctness:**
1. At **< 1000 notes**, the full re-embedding completes within **minutes (not days)** — see Dev Notes → "Performance vs. rate-limit reconciliation" for how this coexists with the 100/day default cap.
2. After completion, the user's **vector search** (`api.embed.searchNotes`) and **related notes** (`notes.getRelatedNotes`) reflect the **new embeddings** immediately (patching `notes.embedding` auto-updates the `by_embedding` vector index — no separate reindex step).

---

stories.title: Nightly Clustering and Auto-MOC Generation
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story-4.1`. Each maps to tasks below.

**AC-1 — Cron configuration**
> **Given** the cron system is configured
> **When** `convex/crons.ts` is created
> **Then** the `nightly-clustering` cron is defined to run at 3 AM user-local time (UTC offset computed from `userSettings.timezone`)
> **And** crons are staggered: clustering at 3 AM, digest at 4 AM — NOT chained (each independently retryable)
> **And** cron names use kebab-case (`"nightly-clustering"`, `"daily-digest"`)

**AC-2 — Loading embeddings + scale strategy**
> **Given** the `runNightlyClustering` internal action fires
> **When** it loads the user's note embeddings
> **Then** it reads all notes with embeddings via paginated queries (not all-at-once)
> **And** for <5K notes: computes pairwise cosine similarity in-memory
> **And** for 5K-50K notes: uses Convex vector search to find top-50 neighbors per note (O(n×50) instead of O(n²))

**AC-3 — Adjacency graph + cluster extraction**
> **Given** cosine similarities are computed
> **When** the adjacency graph is built
> **Then** an edge exists between two notes where cosine similarity exceeds 0.70 threshold
> **And** connected components are found via BFS/DFS traversal
> **And** clusters with fewer than 3 notes are discarded
> **And** clusters with more than 100 notes are split into sub-clusters

**AC-4 — Match against existing MOCs**
> **Given** valid clusters are identified
> **When** they are compared against existing MOCs
> **Then** if a cluster matches an existing MOC (significant overlap in note IDs): the MOC's `noteIds` are updated and `clusterVersion` is incremented
> **And** if a cluster is novel (no matching existing MOC): a new MOC document is created

**AC-5 — New MOC creation + title generation + audit**
> **Given** a new MOC is created
> **When** the title needs to be generated
> **Then** an LLM call (a free OpenRouter model per the free-model policy; default `qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free`) synthesizes a title from the cluster's note contents
> **And** the MOC is stored with: `userId`, `title`, `noteIds`, `confidence` (cluster's internal similarity score), `status: "suggested"`, `clusterVersion: 1`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
> **And** an `auditLog` entry is created with `action: "moc_generated"` (cross-cut: FR43)

**AC-6 — Existing MOC update + audit**
> **Given** an existing MOC is updated
> **When** its note membership changes
> **Then** the `noteIds` array is updated, `clusterVersion` incremented, `updatedAt` refreshed
> **And** an `auditLog` entry is created with `action: "moc_updated"`

**AC-7 — Idempotency**
> **Given** the clustering job is evaluated for correctness
> **When** it runs multiple times with the same data
> **Then** it produces the same results (idempotent — no duplicate MOCs, NFR21)
> **And** the `clusterVersion` field prevents duplicate processing

**AC-8 — Performance**
> **Given** the clustering job is evaluated for performance
> **When** it runs at personal scale
> **Then** it completes within 60 seconds for up to 100K notes (NFR7)

**AC-9 — Failure handling + graceful degradation**
> **Given** the clustering job fails mid-execution
> **When** a retry occurs
> **Then** auto-retry with exponential backoff handles the failure (NFR22)
> **And** the daily digest (Story 4.3) still runs with yesterday's MOC data (graceful degradation)

**Governing requirements:** FR28, FR29, FR30, FR31 (clustering/MOC generation); FR42 (confidence on every MOC); FR43 (audit every AI decision); NFR7 (<60 s / 100K notes); NFR14 (per-user isolation); NFR21 (idempotent); NFR22 (auto-retry backoff); NFR41 (never log note content). [Source: prd.md#FR28–FR43, #NFR7/14/21/22/41]

---

stories.title: MOC Browsing & Curation
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story-4.2`. Each maps to tasks below.

**AC-1 — MOC list grouped by status**
> **Given** auto-MOCs exist from nightly clustering
> **When** the user views the MOC list
> **Then** MOCs are displayed grouped by status: "Suggested" (AI-generated, not yet curated) and "Promoted" (user-confirmed permanent collections)
> **And** each MOC card shows: title, note count, confidence indicator ("AI thinks these belong together" label for suggested), and a preview of 2-3 note snippets
> **And** MOCs are sorted by `updatedAt` descending (most recently updated first)

**AC-2 — Introductory language for first MOC**
> **Given** the user views a suggested MOC introduced with the microcopy
> **When** it is the first MOC the user sees
> **Then** the introductory language is: "I noticed something — these ideas might belong together"

**AC-3 — MOC detail: note list**
> **Given** the user opens a MOC detail
> **When** the MOC's notes are displayed
> **Then** all notes in the MOC are listed as cards with category chips, text previews, and timestamps
> **And** each note has a "×" remove button
> **And** tapping a note card navigates to the note detail (Story 3.2)

**AC-4 — Remove note from MOC**
> **Given** the user taps "×" on a note in a MOC
> **When** the note is removed
> **Then** the note is removed from the MOC's `noteIds` array
> **And** the MOC is updated (no confirmation needed — removal is reversible via next clustering run)
> **And** if the MOC drops below 3 notes, it is automatically archived

**AC-5 — Inline rename**
> **Given** the user wants to rename a MOC
> **When** they tap the title (or an edit icon)
> **Then** the title becomes editable inline
> **And** pressing Enter or tapping away saves the new title
> **And** the rename is stored in Convex

**AC-6 — Promote to permanent**
> **Given** the user wants to promote a suggested MOC
> **When** they tap "Promote" (or equivalent action)
> **Then** the MOC's `status` changes from `"suggested"` to `"promoted"`
> **And** promoted MOCs persist across clustering runs (not overwritten)

**AC-7 — Dismiss MOC**
> **Given** the user wants to dismiss a suggested MOC
> **When** they tap "×" on the MOC card or a "Dismiss" action
> **Then** the MOC's `status` changes to `"archived"`
> **And** archived MOCs do not appear in the list
> **And** no confirmation is needed (dismissal is reversible — the MOC can resurface if clustering data warrants it)

**AC-8 — Empty state / Dark Night teaser**
> **Given** no MOCs exist yet (early usage, <15 captures)
> **When** the MOC section is viewed
> **Then** the empty state shows: "Your ideas are starting to cluster — a suggested collection is forming" (connections-forming teaser for the Dark Night period)

**AC-9 — Accessibility**
> **Given** MOC components are evaluated for accessibility
> **When** the MOC list and detail are inspected
> **Then** the MOC list uses `role="list"` with `aria-label="Knowledge collections"`
> **And** each MOC card is focusable and activatable via keyboard
> **And** the remove "×" button has `aria-label="Remove [note title] from this collection"`
> **And** the inline rename input has `aria-label="Collection name"` and is keyboard-accessible
> **And** promote/dismiss actions have descriptive `aria-label` text

**Governing requirements:** FR32 (MOC curation), FR33a (connections-forming teaser), FR42 (confidence on every MOC), NFR14 (per-user isolation), NFR29 (4.5:1 contrast), NFR30 (focus indicators), NFR31 (reduced motion), NFR32 (44px touch targets), NFR33 (ARIA live regions). [Source: prd.md#FR32/FR33a/FR42, epics.md#Story-4.2]

stories.title: Daily Digest Synthesis
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story-4.3`. Each maps to tasks below.

**AC-1 — Cron fires → worthiness evaluated first**
> **Given** the `daily-digest` cron fires at 4 AM user-local time
> **When** the `generateDailyDigest` internal action executes
> **Then** it first evaluates whether a digest is worth generating (intelligent silence algorithm)

**AC-2 — Intelligent silence algorithm (the four worthiness checks)**
> **Given** the intelligent silence algorithm runs
> **When** it evaluates digest worthiness
> **Then** it checks: (1) any new MOCs since last digest? (2) any existing MOCs updated with new notes? (3) any cross-category connections with similarity >0.75 involving notes from different days? (4) any stalled projects (category with no new captures in 7+ days, configurable)?
> **And** if ALL checks return empty → NO digest is generated, NO notification is sent (FR36)
> **And** silence preserves the signal-to-noise ratio — when a notification DOES arrive, the user knows it's worth opening

**AC-3 — Surprise heuristic (content filter on connections)**
> **Given** the surprise heuristic runs on potential digest content
> **When** connections are evaluated for inclusion
> **Then** connections where BOTH notes are in the same category AND captured on the same day are FILTERED OUT (expected, low-value) (FR37)
> **And** connections spanning different categories OR different weeks are INCLUDED (surprising, high-value)

**AC-4 — Stalled project detection + proactivity profile**
> **Given** stalled project detection runs
> **When** a category has had no new captures for 7+ consecutive days
> **Then** a stalled project nudge is included in the digest content
> **And** the nudge follows the proactivity profile: Coached profiles get nudges, Zen profiles do not (FR35, FR39)

**AC-5 — Content synthesis (LLM)**
> **Given** the digest is worth generating
> **When** the content synthesis occurs
> **Then** a call to a free OpenRouter model (per the free-model policy; default `qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free`) synthesizes a natural-language digest from: filtered connections, new/updated MOCs, stalled project data
> **And** the digest is structured into sections: "Connections forming" (new cross-category links), "Follow-ups" (time-sensitive items, person mentions), "This week's theme" (suggested MOC)
> **And** the digest follows the 70/30 ratio: ~70% familiar captures (reinforcement), ~30% surprising connections (discovery)

**AC-6 — Storage + audit + notification scheduling**
> **Given** the digest is generated
> **When** it is stored
> **Then** a `digests` document is created with: `userId`, `type: "daily"`, `content: { sections: [...], summary }`, `isRead: false`, `createdAt`
> **And** an `auditLog` entry is created with `action: "digest_generated"`
> **And** a push notification is queued for the user's configured morning time via `scheduler.runAt(morningTimeUTC, internal.notifications.sendPushNotification, { digestId })`

**AC-7 — First-digest quality gate**
> **Given** the first digest is generated
> **When** determining if enough data exists
> **Then** the first digest waits until 3+ captures exist to ensure quality (no "Day 1 digest with 1 note")

**AC-8 — Weekly review is CUT (no weekly cron)**
> **Given** FR34 (weekly review) is on the CUT LIST
> **When** the cron system is designed
> **Then** no weekly review cron is created for MVP
> **And** the architecture supports adding it later without changes to the daily digest pipeline

**AC-9 — Idempotency + LLM-failure handling**
> **Given** the digest synthesis is evaluated for reliability
> **When** it runs
> **Then** the job is idempotent — running it twice for the same day produces only one digest (NFR21)
> **And** if the LLM call fails, the digest is not generated (no partial/broken digests) and retry occurs

**Governing requirements:** FR33 (daily digest synthesis), FR35 (stalled-project nudges), FR36 (intelligent silence), FR37 (surprise heuristic), FR39 (adapt to proactivity profile), FR43 (audit every AI decision); NFR14 (per-user isolation), NFR21 (idempotent jobs — no duplicate digests), NFR22 (auto-retry backoff), NFR41 (never log note content). The <10 s digest-synthesis latency budget is architecture-stated, not an NFR. [Source: prd.md#FR33-FR43; architecture.md#Decision 6.4, line 115]

stories.title: Digest Display & Interactivity
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story-4.4` (lines 1421–1490), regrouped into labeled ACs. Each maps to tasks below.

**AC-1 — DigestLayout renders the header + content-bearing sections**
> **Given** the user navigates to the Digest tab
> **When** a digest exists for today (or the most recent unread digest)
> **Then** the DigestLayout renders with the header: "Here's what's connecting in your brain today" (`text-2xl` semibold)
> **And** the digest is organized into sections, each appearing only when it has content: "Connections forming" / "Follow-ups" / "This week's theme"

**AC-2 — Section + card styling, intelligence ordering**
> **Given** a digest section is rendered
> **When** the section content is displayed
> **Then** section headers use `text-sm muted-foreground` with uppercase styling
> **And** digest cards use tighter spacing than the capture thread: `p-3` internal padding, `gap-4` between cards
> **And** each card shows: one-line summary, category chip, and one-tap action to explore
> **And** content is ordered by intelligence (themes, connections, urgency) — never chronologically

**AC-3 — Note deep-link (Story 3.2)**
> **Given** the user taps a digest item referencing a note
> **When** the navigation occurs
> **Then** the note detail opens (Sheet on mobile, detail panel on desktop) — Story 3.2 (FR40)

**AC-4 — MOC deep-link (Story 4.2)**
> **Given** the user taps a digest item referencing a MOC
> **When** the navigation occurs
> **Then** the MOC detail opens (Story 4.2) showing the collection's notes (FR40)

**AC-5 — Stalled-project nudge response (FR41)**
> **Given** a stalled-project nudge appears in the digest
> **When** the user reads the nudge (e.g., "Dashboard v2 hasn't had activity in 9 days. Still active, or should it move to the back burner?")
> **Then** the user can respond with one of three actions: "Still active" (dismiss nudge), "Paused" (mute future nudges for this topic), or "Archived" (stop tracking) (FR41)
> **And** the response is stored and affects future nudge generation

**AC-6 — Empty state: < 3 captures**
> **Given** the user has fewer than 3 captures
> **When** the Digest tab is opened
> **Then** the empty state shows: "Capture a few thoughts first — I'll start finding connections after 3+ captures."
> **And** a CTA button "Go to Capture →" navigates to the Capture tab

**AC-7 — Empty state: 3+ captures, no digest content**
> **Given** the user has 3+ captures but no meaningful digest content
> **When** the Digest tab is opened with no generated digest
> **Then** the empty state shows: "Your ideas are growing — I'll surface connections when patterns emerge."
> **And** no CTA — patience is the action

**AC-8 — Dark Night connections-forming teaser**
> **Given** the user is in the Dark Night period (Week 2-3: 10+ captures accumulated but no auto-MOC has materialized yet)
> **When** the user views the Digest tab or (desktop) detail panel
> **Then** a subtle connections-forming teaser is displayed: "Your ideas are starting to cluster — a suggested collection is forming."
> **And** the teaser appears once the user has ≥10 captures but no MOCs, and disappears once the first MOC is generated

**AC-9 — Loading skeletons**
> **Given** the digest is loading
> **When** data has not yet arrived
> **Then** shaped skeleton cards with section header outlines are displayed with slow 2s pulse
> **And** skeletons appear only after 200ms delay

**AC-10 — Mark as read**
> **Given** the user opens the digest
> **When** the digest is marked as read
> **Then** the `isRead` field on the digest document is set to `true`

**AC-11 — Accessibility**
> **Given** the digest is evaluated for accessibility
> **When** the layout is inspected
> **Then** sections use `role="region"` with `aria-label="[section name]"`
> **And** the digest container has `aria-label="Daily digest"`
> **And** all cards are keyboard-navigable and activatable via Enter
> **And** stalled-project response buttons have descriptive `aria-label` text
> **And** color contrast meets 4.5:1 minimum
> **And** all touch targets ≥ 44x44px

**Governing requirements:** FR40 (deep-link navigation from digest to note/MOC), FR41 (stalled-project nudge response), FR43 (audit AI decisions — N/A for read-only display; nudge responses are user actions, not AI decisions, so no audit row required), FR33a (Dark Night connections-forming teaser); NFR14 (per-user isolation — every new query/mutation is `authenticatedQuery`/`authenticatedMutation`, never accepts `userId`), NFR41 (never log note content). UX: the <30 s scan target, system voice, section taxonomy. [Source: epics.md#Story-4.4; prd.md#FR40-FR41; ux-design-specification.md]

stories.title: Push Notifications & Proactivity Engine
stories.acMarkdown:
Verbatim from `epics.md#Story-4.5` (lines 1499–1538), regrouped into labeled ACs. Each maps to tasks below.

**AC-1 — Web Push + VAPID configured**
> **Given** the push notification system is set up
> **When** Web Push API + VAPID is configured
> **Then** VAPID keys are generated via `web-push generate-vapid-keys` and stored as Convex env vars (`VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY`, `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY`)
> **And** the `web-push` npm package is available in Convex actions

**AC-2 — Subscription prompt (after first capture, never in onboarding)**
> **Given** the user has completed their first successful capture (not during onboarding)
> **When** the push subscription prompt appears
> **Then** a non-blocking toast or banner requests notification permission: "Want to receive your daily digest? You can change this anytime."
> **And** if the user grants permission, the push subscription endpoint is stored in `userSettings.pushSubscription` as a serialized JSON string
> **And** if the user dismisses, the prompt reappears after the 5th capture (never blocks capture flow)
> **And** no permission prompt appears during onboarding

**AC-3 — Scheduled delivery at the user's morning time (NFR11)**
> **Given** a digest has been generated (Story 4.3)
> **When** the push notification is scheduled
> **Then** the `sendPushNotification` internal action is scheduled via `scheduler.runAt(userMorningTimeUTC)` using the user's `digestTime` and `timezone` from `userSettings`
> **And** the notification is dispatched within 1 minute of the scheduled time (NFR11)

**AC-4 — Notification content + tap target**
> **Given** the notification is sent
> **When** the user receives it
> **Then** the notification shows: "Your daily digest is ready. [N] new connection[s] discovered." (or similar concise summary)
> **And** tapping the notification opens the app directly to the Digest tab

**AC-5 — Proactivity engine (per-profile behavior, FR39)**
> **Given** the proactivity engine evaluates notification behavior
> **When** the user's profile is checked
> **Then** Zen: daily digest notification only (no nudges, no stalled project alerts)
> **And** Balanced: daily digest + gentle reminders (stalled projects after 14+ days)
> **And** Coached: daily digest + active nudges (stalled projects after 7+ days, "capture drought" after 3 days)
> **And** Custom: user-configurable combination of the above behaviors (FR39)

**AC-6 — Delivery failure is graceful**
> **Given** the push subscription fails or expires
> **When** the notification cannot be delivered
> **Then** the failure is logged via `console.error`
> **And** the digest remains accessible in-app on the Digest tab (push is a convenience, not a requirement)

**AC-7 — Multi-user-ready architecture (documented, not implemented)**
> **Given** the notification system is evaluated for the future multi-user path
> **When** the architecture is reviewed
> **Then** MVP uses `scheduler.runAt()` with per-user calculated timestamps from the nightly digest job
> **And** future multi-user: global cron triggers fan-out that schedules individual `runAt()` per user (documented, not implemented)

**Governing requirements:** FR38 (push notifications at user-configured times), FR39 (adapt notifications to proactivity profile), FR40 (navigate from notification → Digest tab); NFR11 (push dispatched within 1 min of schedule), NFR14 (per-user isolation — every new query/mutation is `authenticatedQuery`/`authenticatedMutation` or an internal user-scoped query; never accepts a `userId` from a client), NFR41 (never log note content — push logs carry ids/status/error strings only), NFR22 (graceful degradation — a failed push never breaks the digest). UX: "push notifications are invitations, not guilt trips"; "capture is sacred — nothing interrupts the capture flow." [Source: epics.md#Story-4.5; prd.md#FR38-FR40; architecture.md#Decision-6.5; ux-design-specification.md L123,L220,L286]
