stories.title: Audit Trail Browser

criteria.title: List renders (entry point: Settings → Audit Trail)

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-5.1` (lines 1548–1595). Transcribed faithfully; **(the parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**.

**AC1 — List renders (entry point: Settings → Audit Trail)**
- **Given** the user navigates to Settings → Audit Trail, **When** the browser renders, **Then** it displays all `auditLog` entries for the user, sorted by `createdAt` **descending** (newest first).
- **And** each entry shows: timestamp (formatted via `Intl.DateTimeFormat`), action type as a **human-readable label** ("Classification", "Correction", "Embedding", "Collection Generated", "Collection Updated", "Digest Generated"), referenced note (truncated text preview when `noteId` exists), and key details.

**AC2 — Classification entry detail**
- **Given** a classification entry, **Then** it shows: assigned category, **numeric confidence** (e.g. "92%"), the model id used (e.g. `qwen/qwen3-next-80b-a3b-instruct:free`), and the AI's reasoning text.
- **And** this is the **ONLY** place in the app where numeric confidence percentages are shown (main UI uses visual variants only — do not regress that rule elsewhere).

**AC3 — Correction entry detail**
- **Given** a correction entry, **Then** it shows: previous category → new category, the **original** confidence (what the AI assigned before the user corrected), and the timestamp of the correction.

**AC4 — Filtering**
- **Given** the user wants to filter, **Then** filters are available for: **action type** (classification, correction, embedding, moc_generated, …), **date range** (from/to), **category**, and **specific note**.
- **And** filters can be combined (e.g. "all corrections in the last 7 days for category Projects").
- **And** the query uses the `by_user_action` and `by_note` indexes for efficient filtering (see Dev Notes — index-selection strategy; the category predicate is the one documented exception).

**AC5 — Pagination / scale**
- **Given** many entries, **Then** pagination or infinite scroll is implemented (do **not** load all entries at once).
- **And** loading states use **skeleton cards** consistent with the app's loading pattern.

**AC6 — Note deep-link**
- **Given** the user taps an audit entry referencing a note, **Then** the note detail opens (Story 3.2). **Reuse** the existing `?note=` overlay (see Dev Notes — do NOT build a new viewer; mind the param collision).

**AC7 — Empty state**
- **Given** the audit trail is empty (new user, no captures), **Then** the empty state shows exactly: **"AI decisions will appear here as you capture thoughts."**

**AC8 — Accessibility**
- **Given** the browser is evaluated for a11y, **Then** the audit list uses proper `<table>` semantics with column headers (`<th>`) and `aria-sort` on sortable columns.
- **And** filter controls have visible labels and are keyboard-operable.
- **And** all entries are keyboard-navigable (Tab between rows, Enter to open the note).
- **And** color contrast meets 4.5:1 minimum.
- **And** **long-press on mobile / right-click on desktop** from any classified card in the capture thread also opens the audit trail **filtered to that note** ("Why this category?" contextual access).

stories.title: Correction Rate Trends & Trust Metrics

criteria.title: Trend visualization renders (entry point: Settings → Audit Trail, or a dedicated "Trust Metrics" section)
Correction-history data model supports future learning (FR46)

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-5.2` (lines 1599–1645). Transcribed faithfully; **(the parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**.

**AC1 — Trend visualization renders (entry point: Settings → Audit Trail, or a dedicated "Trust Metrics" section)**
- **Given** the user navigates to the Trust Metrics section, **When** the correction-trends view renders, **Then** a trend visualization shows the correction rate over time.
- **And** the correction rate is `corrections / total_classifications` per time period (**weekly** buckets — see Dev Notes for the bucketing decision).
- **And** the trend is a **simple declining line or bar chart** showing the rate dropping over time (e.g. 15% → 8% → 4.2%). (Built as a lightweight inline **SVG** — do NOT add a charting library; see Dev Notes trap #1.)

**AC2 — Rate computed from `auditLog`**
- **Given** the rate is calculated, **When** the data is queried from `auditLog`, **Then** classifications are counted by filtering `action: "classification"` grouped by time period.
- **And** corrections are counted by filtering `action: "correction"` grouped by time period.
- **And** the rate is `corrections / classifications` (displayed as **correction rate** — falling is good). (Accuracy = `1 - rate`; pick one consistent framing in the UI — this story displays **correction rate** to match the "declining line" narrative.)

**AC3 — Summary metrics**
- **Given** summary metrics are displayed, **When** the trust-metrics section renders, **Then** it shows: **total captures classified**, **total corrections made**, **current correction rate (last 7 days)**, and **overall trend direction** (improving / stable / declining).
- (Trend direction semantics: **improving = correction rate falling**. See the aria-label example in AC7.)

**AC4 — Confidence display consistency verified (FR42)**
- **Given** confidence scores are verified across the app, **When** all AI-generated outputs are reviewed, **Then** every classification card displays confidence via **`ConfidenceBadge`** (verified from Epic 2).
- **And** every MOC displays a confidence indicator ("AI thinks these belong together" with visual treatment per confidence level, from Epic 4).
- **And** the audit trail (Story 5.1) shows numeric confidence for detailed inspection.
- **And** the consistency of confidence display across all surfaces is **verified** (this is a VERIFY task — assert via tests; do NOT refactor existing surfaces — see Dev Notes trap #6).

**AC5 — Correction-history data model supports future learning (FR46)**
- **Given** the correction-history data model supports future learning, **When** the data model is verified, **Then** every correction in `auditLog` has: `previousCategory`, `newCategory`, `confidence` (original), `createdAt` (timestamp).
- **And** this structure supports a future decay algorithm (recent corrections weighted higher) **without schema changes**.
- **And** corrections are queryable by recency (the `by_user_action` index with trailing `createdAt` enables this). (VERIFY task — already true from Story 2.4; assert it, change nothing.)

**AC6 — Insufficient-data empty state**
- **Given** not enough data exists for meaningful trends (**< 20 classifications**, lifetime total), **When** the trends view renders, **Then** the empty state shows exactly: **"Keep capturing — I'll show accuracy trends after 20+ classifications."**
- **And** **no chart is shown** (prevents a misleading visualization from a tiny sample).

**AC7 — Accessibility**
- **Given** the trends are evaluated for a11y, **When** the visualization is inspected, **Then** the chart has a **text-based alternative** (e.g. a table of values below the chart).
- **And** the chart uses `role="img"` with an `aria-label` summarizing the trend (e.g. "Correction rate: 15% in week 1, 8% in week 2, 4% in week 3 — improving").
- **And** colors used in the chart are **not the sole indicator** (line/point labels, data-point annotations).

stories.title: Circuit Breakers & Absence Tolerance

criteria.title: Three circuit breakers evaluated during daily digest synthesis
Circuit Breaker 1: Capture Drought → gentle re-engagement nudge
Circuit Breaker 2: Trust Erosion → informational audit alert
Circuit Breaker 3: Nudge Fatigue → automatic profile downshift
Design-for-restart / Absence Tolerance (FR48) — VERIFY across the whole app

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-5.3` (lines 1649–1704). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**. The three breakers are evaluated inside the **existing** daily-digest cron pipeline (Story 4.3 `generateDailyDigest` → `digestForUser`); the pure detection logic lives in `convex/lib/` as reusable, testable units.

**AC1 — Three circuit breakers evaluated during daily digest synthesis**
- **Given** the circuit-breaker detection logic is implemented, **When** the daily digest evaluation runs (integrated with Story 4.3's pipeline), **Then** all three circuit breakers below are evaluated each cron run.
- (They are **additional checks inside the existing `digestForUser`** — NOT a new cron, NOT a new public action. Pure detection functions live in `convex/lib/circuitBreakers.ts`; the DB reads/writes are internal queries/mutations added to `convex/digest.ts`. See Dev Notes "Integration map".)

**AC2 — Circuit Breaker 1: Capture Drought → gentle re-engagement nudge**
- **Given** the user has captured **fewer than 1 note/day for 3+ consecutive days** (operationalized as: **no capture within `captureDroughtDays` days**, the proactivity-config threshold — see trap #2), **When** the drought is detected, **Then** a re-engagement nudge is included in the next digest **if the proactivity profile allows it**, with the **exact** copy: **"Haven't heard from you in a few days — ready when you are"**.
- **And** the nudge is **gentle, not guilt-inducing** (consistent with "absence is not failure"). It is **code-generated deterministic copy** (NOT LLM prose — guarantees the tone) and **rate-limited** so a prolonged drought does not re-nudge daily (see trap #3 — the cadence guard).
- **And** **Zen profiles do NOT receive this nudge; Balanced and Coached profiles DO.** (This requires a config change — see trap #1: today only Coached has a non-null `captureDroughtDays`. Set Balanced's to `3` so it matches the AC. Custom follows Balanced for MVP.)

**AC3 — Circuit Breaker 2: Trust Erosion → informational audit alert**
- **Given** the **correction rate exceeds 20%** over any **rolling 7-day window**, **When** the threshold is breached, **Then** an alert is logged in the audit trail with **`action: "trust_erosion_alert"`**.
- **And** the alert is **visible in the Audit Trail Browser (Story 5.1)** for the user (developer) to investigate.
- **And** the system does **NOT** automatically change classification behavior — the alert is **informational only**, prompting the developer to retune prompts or switch models.
- (`rate = corrections / classifications` over the last 7 days, both row-counts, mirroring Story 5.2's definition. "Exceeds 20%" → strict `> 0.20`; exactly 20% does NOT fire. Apply a brand-new-user sample guard — see trap #4. De-dupe so the alert is not re-logged every day the rate stays high — see trap #5.)

**AC4 — Circuit Breaker 3: Nudge Fatigue → automatic profile downshift**
- **Given** **3+ consecutive digests** have been delivered but **remain unread** (`isRead: false`), **When** nudge fatigue is detected, **Then** the user's proactivity profile is **automatically downshifted one level**: **Coached → Balanced → Zen**.
- **And** the downshift is logged in the audit trail with **`action: "nudge_fatigue_downshift"`**.
- **And** the user is **NOT notified** about the downshift (no push, no UI — reducing noise is the goal).
- **And** the user can manually re-escalate via Settings (Story 1.4 / Epic 6) — **out of scope here** (no Settings profile-editor UI is built in this story; only the internal downshift path).
- (Downshift **stops at Zen** (cannot go lower). **Custom is NOT downshifted** — its knobs are user-defined, Story 6.4. Re-evaluate only against digests created **after the last downshift** so a single fatigue episode doesn't cascade — see trap #6.)

**AC5 — Design-for-restart / Absence Tolerance (FR48) — VERIFY across the whole app**
- **Given** a user returns after **any** absence (1 day, 2 weeks, 3 months), **When** the app loads, **Then** it presents the **same clean interface as daily use**:
  - **NO** "Welcome back!" message.
  - **NO** accumulated badge counts on any tab.
  - **NO** "You missed X digests" or unread indicators.
  - **NO** backlog or "catch up" interface.
  - **Mobile:** clean Capture tab with auto-focused input + blinking cursor.
  - **Desktop:** Digest tab showing the **ONE** most recent digest (not a list of missed ones), OR a clean Capture tab if no recent digest.
- **And** the design-for-restart principle is **verified across ALL screens and states**.
- (This is a **VERIFY task** — the app already satisfies FR48 (audit confirms it). Assert it via tests; do **NOT** rebuild. See Dev Notes "Design-for-restart — current state". Note the deliberate tension with AC2's drought nudge — see trap #7.)

**AC6 — Integration with existing systems**
- **Given** circuit breakers interact with existing systems, **When** the integration is verified, **Then** capture-drought and nudge-fatigue checks are evaluated **as part of the daily digest synthesis** (Story 4.3) — additional checks in the existing `generateDailyDigest` / `digestForUser`.
- **And** the trust-erosion check runs as a **lightweight query in the same cron cycle**.
- **And** circuit-breaker utility functions live in **`convex/lib/`** as **reusable, testable units**.

**AC7 — Correctness / boundary tests**
- **Given** circuit-breaker logic is evaluated for correctness, **When** tests are run, **Then** tests verify: capture-drought detection at **exactly 3 days** (boundary), trust-erosion at **exactly 20%** (boundary — does NOT fire), nudge-fatigue at **exactly 3 unread digests** (boundary).
- **And** tests verify: profile downshift **stops at Zen** (cannot downshift below Zen).
- **And** tests verify: circuit breakers do **NOT** fire for **brand-new users with insufficient data**.

stories.title: Category Management with Usage Stats
stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-6.1` (lines 1712–1756). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**. This is the **first** story of Epic 6 (Advanced Knowledge Organization) and the first user-facing **write** surface for the category taxonomy created at onboarding (Story 1.3). It is a **Settings** concern, not a capture concern (architecture.md:53).

**AC1 — Category list with usage stats**
- **Given** the user navigates to **Settings → Categories**, **When** the category management page renders, **Then** all user-defined categories are listed with: **category name** (colored swatch derived from its HSL hue), **note count**, **percentage of total captures**, and the category's **left-border color swatch**.
- **And** categories are **sorted by `sortOrder`** (matching the order from onboarding).
- (Note count comes from the **existing** `notes.getCategoryNoteCounts` query — do NOT write a new counter. Percentage = `categoryCount / sum(all category counts)` computed client-side, guarding divide-by-zero → `0%`. See Dev Notes "Usage stats — reuse, don't rebuild". The "name colored with HSL color" means a **swatch/border** in the hue — **never render the category text itself in the hue** (`colors.ts` contract: hues are decorative, 3:1 non-text only; text uses Stone `--foreground`).)

**AC2 — Create a category (inline)**
- **Given** the user wants to create a new category, **When** they tap **"Add Category"** (or equivalent action), **Then** a new inline input field appears.
- **And** upon entering a name and confirming, a new `categories` document is created with **`isAISuggested: false`**.
- **And** the HSL color generation regenerates colors for **ALL** categories (even distribution across the color wheel changes when `n` changes).
- **And** all existing note cards update their left-border and chip colors to reflect the new distribution.
- (**TRAP — the AC says "entering a name" but the schema REQUIRES a non-empty `description`** and the classifier injects descriptions into its prompt — a description-less category "cannot be used by the classifier" (epics.md:377). The inline create form MUST collect **both a name and a description** (description required, mirroring `createCategories`' server validation). See trap #1. The "regenerate colors for ALL categories" + "note cards update" behavior is **automatic and FREE** via the reactive `getCategories` query — do NOT persist colors, do NOT write a recolor routine. See trap #3.)

**AC3 — Rename a category (inline)**
- **Given** the user wants to rename a category, **When** they tap the category name (or an edit icon), **Then** the name becomes editable inline.
- **And** pressing **Enter** or tapping away **saves** the new name.
- **And** the rename **propagates to all views** where the category name appears (capture thread, browse, digest).
- (Propagation to **live** views is automatic — every surface reads the name through the reactive `getCategories` query; notes store `categoryId`, never a denormalized name. The ONE exception: already-generated `digests` store a denormalized `category` name **string** snapshot (`schema.ts:123`) — historical digests keep the old name; NEW digests use the new name. This point-in-time snapshot staleness is **acceptable** — do NOT rewrite historical digest content. See trap #4. Rename edits the **name only** in this story; description editing is out of scope (deferred to Settings hub, Story 6.4).)

**AC4 — Delete a category (with confirmation + reclassification)**
- **Given** the user wants to delete a category, **When** they tap a delete action, **Then** a confirmation dialog appears: **"Delete [Category Name]? [N] captures will become uncategorized."**
- **And** the confirm button uses **`--destructive`** color and is labeled **"Delete"** (not generic "OK").
- **And** upon confirmation, the category document is deleted.
- **And** all notes with this `categoryId` have their **`categoryId` set to `undefined`** and **`status` set to `"pending"`** (re-classification triggered).
- **And** HSL colors **regenerate** for remaining categories.
- (`[N]` is the deleted category's note count (`getCategoryNoteCounts[id]`, or recompute in the mutation). **Re-classification gate (trap #2):** schedule `internal.classify.classifyNote` per affected note **only if ≥1 category remains** after deletion — mirror `createNote`'s defensive 0-categories guard (`notes.ts:31-42`). Deleting the **last** category leaves notes `pending` + uncategorized with nothing scheduled. Color regeneration is again automatic via reactive `getCategories`. Mirror the **`DeleteNoteDialog`** a11y pattern exactly (trap #5).)

**AC5 — Loading skeleton**
- **Given** the category management page is loading, **When** data is being fetched, **Then** **skeleton rows** matching the expected category-row layout are displayed with a **slow 2s pulse**.
- (Reuse the `Skeleton` primitive (`components/ui/skeleton.tsx`) whose Tailwind `animate-pulse` IS the global ~2s pulse — mirror `NoteCardSkeleton.tsx`. The query is "loading" when `useQuery` returns `undefined`.)

**AC6 — Accessibility**
- **Given** the category management is evaluated for accessibility, **When** the page is inspected, **Then**:
  - the category list uses **semantic list markup** (`<ul>`/`<li>`) with **`aria-label="Your categories"`**;
  - edit controls are **keyboard-accessible** (Tab to navigate, **Enter** to edit/confirm, **Escape** to cancel);
  - the delete confirmation uses **`role="alertdialog"`** with **`aria-describedby`**;
  - color swatches are **accompanied by text labels** (color is **never** the sole indicator);
  - all interactive elements have **visible focus indicators** (`focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`).

stories.title: AI Category Suggestions

criteria.title: Generate a suggestion only on sustained evidence

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-6.2` (lines 1760–1804). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**. This is the **second** story of Epic 6 and the project's **first AI-initiated taxonomy write** — the AI proposes, the user disposes (UX spec line 211: "AI suggested → You confirmed" collaboration narrative). It builds on Story 6.1's category-management surface (the suggestion card renders inside Settings → Categories). **Conservative by design** — the whole point is to NOT proliferate categories (PRD line 265: "It would rather classify into existing categories imperfectly than fragment the taxonomy… The goal is 4-7 categories total, not 15.").

**AC1 — Generate a suggestion only on sustained evidence**
- **Given** the system monitors capture patterns over time, **When** 20+ captures across multiple weeks (**minimum 3 weeks** of data) consistently don't fit well into existing categories (classification confidence for these notes **averages below 70%**), **Then** the system generates a category suggestion.
- **And** the suggestion is **conservative** — **one suggestion at a time**, no category proliferation.
- (This is a **scheduled background job** — a weekly cron (`internal.categorySuggestions.generateCategorySuggestions`), NOT a per-capture trigger. "Don't fit well" = a note the classifier assigned with **low confidence**: `status ∈ {classified, review}` AND `confidence != null` AND `confidence < 0.70` (the `SUGGESTION_CONFIDENCE_CEILING`). Such notes ARE captured live today — Story 5.3 confirmed the free-OpenRouter classifier runs and writes `confidence`. "One at a time" = the generation **GATE skips if a `pending` suggestion already exists**. See trap #1 + Dev Notes "Suggestion algorithm".)

**AC2 — Suggestion card content + microcopy**
- **Given** a category suggestion is generated, **When** it appears in **Settings → Categories**, **Then** a **suggestion card** is displayed with: **proposed category name**, **evidence summary** ("Over the past 6 weeks, 34 notes about [topic] don't fit your current categories"), **example notes** (3–5 representative captures), and **confidence level**.
- **And** the suggestion uses the microcopy pattern: **not aggressive** ("Consider adding…?" not "You should add…").
- (The evidence summary string is **LLM-generated** and stored on the suggestion row (`evidence` field) — render it verbatim. Example notes' **text** is resolved server-side in the public query (`getPendingSuggestion` does ≤5 `ctx.db.get`s and returns `{_id, content}`) so the card has the captures to display. Confidence renders as `"Confidence: {Math.round(confidence*100)}%"` (the UX example shows "Confidence: 81%", UX spec line 261). Mirror the **`MOCSuggestionCard`** tone/structure — it is the established "AI proposes" card; reuse its dismiss-"×" + intro-microcopy patterns. The intro line is conservative, e.g. **"Consider adding a new category?"** — never imperative.)

**AC3 — Accept the suggestion**
- **Given** the suggestion is displayed, **When** the user wants to accept it, **Then** they tap **"Add [Category Name]"**.
- **And** a new category is created with **`isAISuggested: true`**.
- **And** the system **prompts for retroactive reclassification (Story 6.3)**.
- **And** HSL colors **regenerate** for all categories.
- (Accept calls a dedicated mutation `acceptCategorySuggestion({ suggestionId, name })` — it creates the category with `isAISuggested: **true**` (NOT `createCategory`, which hardcodes `false`), `sortOrder = max+1`, `description = suggestion.suggestedDescription`, and marks the suggestion row `status: "accepted"`. **Color regeneration is automatic/FREE** via the reactive `getCategories` — same as Story 6.1 trap #3; nothing to write. **The "prompts for retroactive reclassification" clause is Story 6.3's flow and is OUT OF SCOPE here** — 6.3 is still `backlog`. In 6.2, accept **creates the category and stops**; do NOT build the batch-reclassify confirmation dialog. See trap #4.)

**AC4 — Reject (dismiss) the suggestion**
- **Given** the suggestion is displayed, **When** the user wants to reject it, **Then** they tap **"Dismiss" (or "×")**.
- **And** the suggestion is dismissed and **will not recur for this topic for at least 4 weeks**.
- **And** **no confirmation is needed** (dismissal is low-risk).
- (Dismiss calls `dismissSuggestion({ suggestionId })` → patches `status: "dismissed"`, `respondedAt: Date.now()`. **No `alertdialog`** — fire immediately (mirror `MOCSuggestionCard`'s no-confirmation dismiss, UX spec line 2003 "Dismiss MOC … No — dismissal is reversible"). The **4-week cooldown** is enforced in the generation GATE: skip if the most-recent dismissed suggestion's `respondedAt` is within `SUGGESTION_COOLDOWN_WEEKS` (28 days). See Dev Notes "Cooldown" — at single-user MVP the cooldown is applied as a **global** post-dismissal pause (only one suggestion is ever live), which is strictly *more* conservative than per-topic; the per-topic nuance is a documented simplification.)

**AC5 — Modify before accepting**
- **Given** the suggestion is displayed, **When** the user wants to modify it, **Then** they can **edit the proposed category name** before accepting.
- **And** acceptance with a modified name creates the category with the user's chosen name and **`isAISuggested: true`**.
- (Inline-edit the name on the card (mirror Story 6.1's `CategoryManager` rename / `MOCTitleEditor` inline-edit: auto-focus, Enter/blur commits the edit into local state, Escape reverts). The edited name is the `name` arg passed to `acceptCategorySuggestion`. `isAISuggested` stays **true** even when the name is user-edited — it was still an AI-originated suggestion. The **description is NOT user-editable** here (the LLM-generated `suggestedDescription` is used as-is; description editing is the Settings-hub/6.4 concern, mirroring 6.1's rename-name-only scope).)

**AC6 — Conservatism thresholds (the algorithm gate)**
- **Given** the suggestion algorithm is evaluated for conservatism, **When** the thresholds are tested, **Then** the algorithm does **NOT** suggest a new category if: **fewer than 20** qualifying notes exist, the qualifying notes span **fewer than 3 weeks**, or the user **already has 7+ categories** (the goal is 4–7 total, not 15).
- (Three hard gates, all in one pure, unit-testable predicate `shouldGenerateSuggestion(...)`: `qualifyingCount >= 20` AND `weeksSpanned >= 3` AND `categoryCount < 7` — plus the AC1/AC4 gates (no existing `pending`, not within the 4-week dismissal cooldown). `weeksSpanned` = `(newestQualifying.createdAt − oldestQualifying.createdAt) / WEEK_MS`. These are the **conservatism contract** — test each boundary first-class. See trap #2.)

**AC7 — Accessibility**
- **Given** the suggestion system is evaluated for accessibility, **When** the suggestion card is inspected, **Then**:
  - the card is **keyboard-navigable** with **accept/reject/modify** actions accessible via **Tab and Enter**;
  - **example notes are read aloud by screen readers as supporting evidence** (render them as real text content, e.g. inside a labelled list/region — not `aria-hidden`);
  - all actions have **descriptive `aria-label`** text (e.g. `Add category ${name}`, `Dismiss category suggestion`, `Edit suggested category name`);
  - all interactive elements have **visible focus indicators** (`focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`).

stories.title: Retroactive Reclassification

criteria.title: Identify candidate notes by a lightweight confidence check
Confirm → batched, idempotent reclassification with audit trail
Decline → category kept, no notes moved

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-6.3` (lines 1808–1849). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**. This is the **third** story of Epic 6 and the **completion of the category-creation loop** opened by 6.1 (manual create) and 6.2 (AI-suggestion accept): both now end with an optional "Reclassify [N] existing notes?" prompt. It is a **confirm-then-act, high-impact-but-reversible** operation (UX spec line 1482, 1903) — a single explicit confirmation, then a **batched, cancellable, idempotent** background move. **No new AI call** — candidate selection is a confidence-threshold filter, not a per-note LLM re-classification (see trap #8).

**AC1 — Identify candidate notes by a lightweight confidence check**
- **Given** a new category has been created (via Story 6.1 manual creation **or** Story 6.2 suggestion acceptance), **When** the system identifies notes that might belong to the new category, **Then** it evaluates existing notes by running a **lightweight classification check**: notes currently classified with **confidence below 75%** in their assigned category are candidates for reclassification.
- (The "lightweight classification check" is a **pure DB threshold filter**, NOT an LLM/embedding re-classification. A **candidate** = a note where `(status ∈ {classified, review})` AND `confidence != null` AND `confidence < RECLASSIFICATION_CONFIDENCE_CEILING (0.75)` AND it has an assigned `categoryId` that is **not** the new category. These are the captures the classifier was *unsure* about — the notes most likely to benefit from a freshly-created category. This is the deliberate MVP interpretation faithful to the AC's literal candidate definition and AC3's "set to a **system-defined value**" — it needs no `GEMINI_API_KEY` and matches the project free-model / no-embeddings posture (memory `project_gemini_api_key_missing`, mirrors 6.2's confidence-based approach). See trap #8 + Dev Notes "Candidate selection".)

**AC2 — Confirmation dialog**
- **Given** candidate notes are identified, **When** the reclassification prompt appears, **Then** a confirmation dialog shows: **"Reclassify [N] existing notes to [New Category Name]?"**
- **And** the dialog explains: **"This will update the category for [N] existing notes."**
- **And** the **Cancel** button is on the **left**, the **"Reclassify"** action button is on the **right** (standard confirmation pattern).
- (Mirror `DeleteCategoryDialog` exactly (`role="alertdialog"`, `aria-describedby` → the description). The confirm button is the **primary** action labelled with the verb **"Reclassify"** (UX line 1921 — verb, never "OK") — **NOT** `--destructive`: reclassification is high-impact but reversible (per-note correction undoes it), so it does **not** use the destructive red (trap #3). Pluralize "note"/"notes" on `N`. The dialog only appears when **N ≥ 1** — a zero-candidate creation is silent, AC1's "When the system identifies notes that might belong" (trap #12). `N` and the category name come from the just-created category — the card/form already holds both.)

**AC3 — Confirm → batched, idempotent reclassification with audit trail**
- **Given** the user confirms reclassification, **When** the batch reclassification executes, **Then** each candidate note's **`categoryId` is updated to the new category**.
- **And** each note's **`confidence` is recalculated or set to a system-defined value (e.g., `0.80` — system-initiated reclassification)**.
- **And** an **`auditLog` entry is created for each reclassified note** with `action: "reclassification"`, `details: { previousCategory, newCategory }`.
- **And** the reclassification process is **idempotent** (running it twice doesn't double-reclassify).
- (Set `confidence = RECLASSIFIED_CONFIDENCE (0.80)` (a flat system value — NO per-note LLM recompute, AC3's "set to a system-defined value") and `status = "classified"` (0.80 clears the default 0.6 threshold and resolves any `review` note out of the review queue — the consistent corollary; trap #9). `action: "reclassification"` is **ALREADY in the `auditLog.action` union** (schema.ts:89) — do **NOT** widen it (trap #4). Write `details: { previousCategory: <note's old categoryId>, newCategory: <job.categoryId>, confidence: 0.80 }`. **Idempotency is structural**: a reclassified note now has `confidence 0.80 (≥ 0.75 ceiling)` and the target `categoryId`, so it **drops out of the candidate filter** — a second run moves nothing (trap #2). No separate "processed" set needed; the note data IS the cursor.)

**AC4 — Decline → category kept, no notes moved**
- **Given** the user declines reclassification, **When** they tap **Cancel**, **Then** the new category is **still created** (from Story 6.1 or 6.2) but **no existing notes are moved**.
- **And** future captures may be classified into the new category normally.
- (The category is created by `createCategory` / `acceptCategorySuggestion` **before** the dialog opens — Cancel simply closes the dialog and clears the trigger state; **no** reclassification job is started. The new category is immediately live for future classification (it is in the user's `categories` set → the classifier's prompt). Cancel must NOT delete or alter the category.)

**AC5 — Batched, progress-tracked, cancellable execution**
- **Given** the reclassification involves many notes, **When** the batch process runs, **Then** it processes notes **in batches** (not all at once) to avoid Convex action timeouts.
- **And** a progress indicator shows **"Reclassifying [X] of [N] notes..."**
- **And** the process **can be cancelled mid-way** (already-reclassified notes remain, unprocessed ones stay in their original category).
- (Implement as a **self-rescheduling `internalMutation`** (`reclassifyBatch`) — reclassification is pure DB work (patch + audit insert, **no `fetch`**), so a **mutation** is correct: each batch is a bounded transaction and there is **no action to time out** (trap #5). Each invocation processes ≤ `RECLASSIFICATION_BATCH_SIZE (50)` candidates, patches the `reclassificationJobs` progress row, and `ctx.scheduler.runAfter(0, …reclassifyBatch, { jobId })` reschedules itself until no candidates remain → `status: "completed"`. The progress indicator reads the reactive `getActiveReclassificationJob` query → renders `Reclassifying {processedCount} of {total} notes…` with `aria-live="polite"` (AC6) + a **Cancel** button → `cancelReclassification({ jobId })` patches `status: "cancelled"`; the in-flight worker checks `status !== "running"` at the **top** of its next batch and stops, leaving processed notes moved and the rest untouched (trap #7). `N` (= `total`) is snapshotted at job start.)

**AC6 — Accessibility**
- **Given** reclassification is evaluated for accessibility, **When** the confirmation dialog is inspected, **Then**:
  - the dialog uses **`role="alertdialog"`** with **`aria-describedby`** linking to the description (mirror `DeleteCategoryDialog`);
  - **focus is trapped** within the dialog (Radix `Dialog` provides this) and returns to the trigger on close;
  - **Escape dismisses** (equivalent to Cancel — Radix `onOpenChange(false)`);
  - the **progress indicator uses `aria-live="polite"`** for updates (a `role="status"` region announcing `Reclassifying X of N notes…`);
  - all interactive elements (Cancel, Reclassify, the progress Cancel) have **visible focus indicators** (`focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`) and descriptive labels.

stories.title: Settings Hub & Profile Modification

criteria.title: Settings hub: organized links with current values + AI API Costs section
Proactivity modification page: the four onboarding cards, active highlighted, change anytime
Selecting a profile updates immediately + toast + next-cycle effect
Confidence threshold control (0.40–0.90), applies to future classifications
Timezone & digest delivery time, with immediate UTC recalculation + push reschedule

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story-6.4` (lines 1853–1907). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**. This is the **final feature story of Epic 6** — it turns the placeholder Settings page (a stub since Story 5.1, `app/(app)/(main)/settings/page.tsx:4-9` literally says "The full hub is Story 6.4") into a real hub and adds the **post-onboarding modification** surface for the four preferences captured once during onboarding (Story 1.4): proactivity profile, confidence threshold, timezone, digest time. **No new schema, no new table, no new cron** — every field already exists on `userSettings` (schema.ts:138–153); this story adds the **update mutations** (none exist yet — onboarding only ever *inserts*) and the **UI to drive them**. **No external-provider dependency** (no GEMINI/VAPID): all four are plain `userSettings` patches read fresh by the pipelines that consume them.

**AC1 — Settings hub: organized links with current values + AI API Costs section**
- **Given** the user navigates to Settings, **When** the settings hub page renders, **Then** it displays organized links to: **Categories** (Story 6.1), **Proactivity Profile** (this story), **Audit Trail** (Story 5.1), **Export** (Story 7.5), **Account** (Story 7.6), and an **"AI API Costs" external-link section**.
- **And** each link shows a **brief description and the current value** (e.g., "Proactivity: Balanced", "Categories: 4").
- **And** the "AI API Costs" section contains **two `target="_blank" rel="noopener"` outbound links**: "OpenRouter dashboard" (classification + digest costs) and the embedding provider's dashboard (e.g. Google AI Studio) (embedding costs) — satisfying **NFR45 via external-dashboard path per architecture Decision 5.3**.
- **And** a brief caption below reads: **"View current-month AI usage and costs in each provider's dashboard"**.
- (**Export (7.5) and Account (7.6) routes DO NOT EXIST** — Epic 7 is backlog. Render them as **disabled "Coming soon" rows**, NOT active links that 404 — trap #3. **PRESERVE the existing "Trust Metrics" link** (Story 5.2, currently in the hub) — the AC list is additive, not exclusive; removing a working link is a 5.2 regression — trap #2. The hub must become a **reactive client surface** to show live current values (`getUserSettings.proactivityProfile` → "Proactivity: <Label>"; `getCategories().length` → "Categories: N") — auth-gate the queries with `isAuthenticated ? {} : "skip"` (mirror `settings/categories/page.tsx`) — trap #12. The two cost links use **representative canonical dashboard URLs** (OpenRouter: `https://openrouter.ai/activity`; Google AI Studio: `https://aistudio.google.com/`); `rel="noopener"` is mandatory (the AC names it). See Dev Notes "Settings hub design".)

**AC2 — Proactivity modification page: the four onboarding cards, active highlighted, change anytime**
- **Given** the user navigates to **Settings → Proactivity**, **When** the proactivity modification page renders, **Then** the **same four profile cards from onboarding** (Story 1.4) are displayed: **Zen, Balanced, Coached, Custom**.
- **And** the **currently active profile is highlighted**.
- **And** **"You can change this anytime"** is shown in muted text (consistent with onboarding).
- (**Reuse the `PROACTIVITY_PROFILES` constant** (`lib/onboarding/proactivityProfiles.ts` — the single source of truth for the 4 cards/labels/descriptions, already used by onboarding) — do NOT re-list the profiles inline (drift). **Mirror the `role="radio"` in `radiogroup` card pattern** from `components/onboarding/ProactivitySelector.tsx` (Radix `RadioGroup` + `RadioGroupPrimitive.Item asChild`, `data-[state=checked]:border-primary`, `Check` icon on the selected). But this is a **NEW component** (`ProactivitySettings.tsx`) — do NOT reuse `ProactivitySelector` directly: it calls `completeOnboarding(profile, timezone)` and has a "Continue" button (onboarding flow); here selection is **immediate** with NO Continue (AC3) and a different mutation — trap #4. "Active" = `settings.proactivityProfile`.)

**AC3 — Selecting a profile updates immediately + toast + next-cycle effect**
- **Given** the user selects a different proactivity profile, **When** they tap a new profile card, **Then** the **`userSettings.proactivityProfile` is updated immediately** via Convex mutation.
- **And** a confirmation **toast appears: "Profile updated to [name]"**.
- **And** the change **takes effect from the next digest cycle** (no retroactive changes to already-scheduled notifications).
- (Call the new `updateProactivityProfile({ proactivityProfile })` mutation on card tap — **no Continue button**. Toast via `@/hooks/use-toast` (`toast({ description: "Profile updated to Balanced" })`; use the profile's **label**, e.g. "Balanced", not the value "balanced"). "Takes effect next digest cycle" requires **NO reschedule work** — the digest pipeline reads `proactivityProfile` **fresh** every 4 AM-UTC run (`internal.digest.getUserDigestSettings`, digest.ts:353–375), so a patch alone is sufficient; do NOT touch any scheduled job for a profile change — trap #9. NOT an AI decision → **no `auditLog` row** (mirror `savePushSubscription` — trap #7). Graceful-failure toast on mutation reject (mirror `CategoryManager`).)

**AC4 — Confidence threshold control (0.40–0.90), applies to future classifications**
- **Given** the user wants to modify the confidence threshold, **When** they access the confidence setting, **Then** the **current threshold is displayed (default 0.60)**.
- **And** the user can adjust it **via a slider or input (range: 0.40 to 0.90)**.
- **And** a description explains: **"Lower = more auto-classification, higher = more items in 'Needs Review'"**.
- **And** the change is **saved to `userSettings.confidenceThreshold`** and **applies to future classifications**.
- (Use a **native `<input type="range" min={0.40} max={0.90} step={0.05}>`** — there is **NO `slider.tsx`** in `components/ui` and **NO `@radix-ui/react-slider` dependency**; do NOT add one for a single control (the codebase's minimal-dependency stance — cf. the 3.2 `vaul` rejection — trap #5). A native range input **automatically exposes `aria-valuemin`/`aria-valuemax`/`aria-valuenow`** via the implicit `role="slider"` (satisfies AC6) — render a visible numeric/percent readout beside it. Save via `updateConfidenceThreshold({ confidenceThreshold })` **on commit** (`onChange` or pointer-up — debounce/commit so you don't fire a mutation per pixel). **Server validates the range** (ConvexError if `< 0.40` or `> 0.90` — security boundary, never trust the client — trap #11). "Applies to future classifications" needs **NO extra work**: `getClassificationContext` reads `confidenceThreshold` fresh per note (classify.ts ~199–305) — a patch is sufficient — trap #10. No audit row.)

**AC5 — Timezone & digest delivery time, with immediate UTC recalculation + push reschedule**
- **Given** the user wants to modify timezone or digest delivery time, **When** they access these settings, **Then** the **current timezone (auto-detected) and digest time (default "08:00") are displayed**.
- **And** the user can **modify both**.
- **And** changes are **saved to `userSettings`** and affect the **next scheduled digest/notification delivery**.
- **Given** the user changes their timezone in settings, **When** the timezone is updated in `userSettings.timezone`, **Then**:
  - the **UTC equivalent of the user's configured digest time is recalculated immediately**;
  - **any scheduled notification delivery (via `scheduler.runAt`) for today's digest is rescheduled to the new UTC timestamp**;
  - the nightly clustering (3 AM local) and daily digest (4 AM local) cron jobs **recalculate their UTC offsets for subsequent runs**;
  - **for MVP single-user: cron schedules are computed per-run from `userSettings.timezone`, not hardcoded** — changing timezone takes effect on the next cron cycle.
- (Implement `updateDigestPreferences({ timezone, digestTime })` (one mutation, both values — the UI sends current values, mirror how `completeOnboarding` sends the full set). **Validate** `digestTime` with `parseHhmm` and `timezone` with `isValidIanaTimeZone` (both exported from `lib/time.ts` in Task 2 — ConvexError on invalid — trap #11). The **"recalculated immediately" + "rescheduled" + "today's pending push"** bullets are the **only genuinely-new scheduling work** → reschedule the caller's pending same-day push (Task 4, trap #6): `nextUtcOccurrenceOfLocalTime(digestTime, timezone, Date.now())` recomputes the UTC instant; find + cancel the caller's pending `sendPushNotification` and re-`runAt` it. **The cron bullets are an architecture-reconciled DESCOPE, NOT a `crons.ts` change** — Convex crons are fixed-UTC, evaluated at deploy time with NO DB access (`crons.ts:6-10`), and per-user-timezone cron firing is the **sanctioned Decision 6.5 multi-user deferral** (Story 4.1 review, deferred-work.md line 99). "Cron schedules computed per-run from `userSettings.timezone` / takes effect next cron cycle" is **already satisfied in spirit** because the digest action **re-reads `userSettings.timezone` fresh** every 4 AM-UTC run and recomputes the per-user delivery time (`digestForUser`, digest.ts:1389–1400). **Do NOT refactor `crons.ts`** — trap #1. See Dev Notes "Timezone change — exact scope".)

**AC6 — Accessibility**
- **Given** the settings hub is evaluated for accessibility, **When** the pages are inspected, **Then**:
  - **all form controls have visible labels and are keyboard-operable**;
  - the proactivity cards **reuse the `role="radio"` in `radiogroup` pattern from onboarding**;
  - **slider/input controls have `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, and `aria-valuenow`** attributes (native `<input type="range">` provides these);
  - **all changes provide confirmation via `role="status"` toast announcements** (the app `Toaster` already renders an `aria-live` region — `components/ui/toaster.tsx`);
  - **color contrast meets 4.5:1 minimum throughout**.
- (Mirror the onboarding `RadioGroup` a11y (focus-visible rings, `Check` icon as a non-color indicator). Native range input keyboard-operates (arrows) and exposes the value attrs automatically. Each settings page gets an **axe** pass (mirror `CategoryManager.test.tsx`); per project convention the jsdom `color-contrast` axe rule is disabled — confirm contrast visually, deferred-work.md line 14.)

stories.title: Offline Capture Queue
stories.acMarkdown:
### Layer 1: Online (default — already working, must not regress)

1. When online, capture continues to use the existing path unchanged: `CaptureThread` fires `api.notes.createNote` with `withOptimisticUpdate`, the card appears instantly (`status:"pending"`, border-pulse), classification is scheduled server-side, and the reactive `getNotes` subscription resolves the card. **No existing Story 2.1/2.3 behavior may break.** (Regression guard.)

### Layer 2: Offline with JavaScript (Service Worker + IndexedDB)

2. **Given** the app is loaded and React has hydrated, **when** the user is offline (no network connectivity), **then** a subtle offline indicator appears (small dot/icon in the status area, **amber**) — not blocking, not alarming — **and** the capture input remains fully functional (type, send).
3. **Given** the user submits a capture while offline, **when** the `createNote` mutation fails due to no connectivity (or `navigator.onLine === false` is detected up front), **then** the failure is caught by the `useOfflineCapture` hook, **and** the capture is stored in IndexedDB with `{ content, createdAt, syncStatus: "queued" }`, **and** a capture card appears in the thread with the microcopy **"Saved locally — I'll classify this when I reconnect"**, **and** the card has **no border pulse** (nothing is pending — it is queued, not classifying), **and** the user can continue capturing more thoughts (all queued in IndexedDB).
4. **Given** offline captures exist in the IndexedDB queue, **when** network connectivity returns, **then** the queue is replayed: each queued capture is submitted to Convex via `api.notes.createNote` **in order (oldest `createdAt` first)**, **and** on successful sync the record's `syncStatus` is updated to `"synced"` in IndexedDB (then pruned — see Dev Notes), **and** the synced cards transition to the live classification state (border pulse starts as classification kicks in), **and** a subtle toast confirms **"N captures synced"** (e.g. "2 captures synced"). The toast uses `role="status"`.
5. **Given** the IndexedDB queue persists across app restarts, **when** the user closes and reopens the app while still offline, **then** previously queued captures are still visible in the thread in the "Saved locally" state (NFR20), **and** when connectivity returns they sync normally (AC4).
6. **Given** the Background Sync API is not available (some browsers / iOS), **when** the app detects no Background Sync support, **then** it falls back to checking connectivity on each app focus / `visibilitychange` and on the `window` `online` event, **and** queued captures sync on the next online detection.

### Layer 3: Pre-Hydration Capture (Static HTML form)

7. **Given** the Service Worker has cached the app shell (full precaching is **Story 7.2** — see cross-story dependency note), **when** the user opens the app and React has **not yet hydrated** (cold launch window), **then** a static HTML `<form>` with a native `<textarea>` and submit button is visible and functional, **and** the form is part of the cached app shell — it works without JavaScript.
8. **Given** the user types and submits via the static HTML form before hydration, **when** the submission is intercepted by the Service Worker's `fetch` event handler, **then** the Service Worker extracts the form data and stores it in IndexedDB (same queue/shape as Layer 2), **and** the form clears / shows a brief confirmation via the Service Worker's synthetic response.
9. **Given** React hydrates after a pre-hydration capture, **when** hydration completes, **then** React reads the IndexedDB queue and reconciles: pre-hydration captures appear in the capture thread, **and** if online they are immediately submitted to Convex, **and** if offline they remain queued alongside any post-hydration offline captures (no duplicates).

### ADR conformance & accessibility

10. **Given** the offline architecture follows the ADR, **when** the implementation is reviewed, **then** Layer 1 (online) is the default path (unchanged from Epic 2), Layer 2 handles typical offline scenarios, Layer 3 handles the cold-launch edge case, **and** each layer is independent and testable.
11. **Given** offline capture is evaluated for accessibility, **when** the offline state is inspected, **then** the offline indicator is **not color-only** (icon + color + accessible text), **and** the "Saved locally" card state is announced via `aria-live="polite"`, **and** the static HTML form (Layer 3) has a proper `<label>` and is keyboard-submittable, **and** the sync toast uses `role="status"`.

stories.title: PWA Installation & App Shell Caching
stories.acMarkdown:
### Manifest & icons

1. **Given** the PWA manifest is configured, **when** `app/manifest.ts` is created, **then** it exports a default function returning a `MetadataRoute.Manifest` with: `name: "xynapse"`, `short_name: "xynapse"`, `display: "standalone"`, `start_url: "/"`, a `theme_color` and `background_color` derived from `--background` (single static value per the manifest spec — per-theme color is handled by the `viewport` `themeColor` media queries in AC4, see Dev Notes), **and** `icons` providing **192x192** and **512x512** PNGs. Next.js auto-injects `<link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.webmanifest">` when this file exists — do **not** add a manual `<link>`.
2. **Given** icons must exist as real files, **when** the manifest references them, **then** valid PNG icons exist at `public/icon-192.png` (192x192) and `public/icon-512.png` (512x512) — placeholder/solid-brand for MVP, branded as later polish. These same files back the Story 4.5 push notification `icon`/`badge` (`app/sw.ts:54-57` already references `/icon-192.png`), which are **currently 404** — this story fixes that.

### Service Worker caching strategy

3. **Given** the Service Worker caching strategy is implemented via Serwist, **when** the caching layers are reviewed, **then**: **(Precached on SW install)** the static app shell — JS chunks (app shell, capture module, navigation), CSS (Tailwind build), the PWA manifest, and app icons — is served from cache for an instant warm launch (already provided by Serwist's `self.__SW_MANIFEST` precache + `defaultCache` runtime caching in `app/sw.ts`; verify, do not rebuild). **(Runtime cached)** offline browsing of recent Convex data is handled **by the Convex client, not the Service Worker** — see Dev Notes → caching reality; the SW must NOT attempt to fetch-cache Convex traffic. **(Network only)** AI classification, embedding, search, and correction mutations are always fresh — naturally satisfied because they ride Convex's transport, which the SW never intercepts.
4. **Given** the PWA must launch correctly themed and in standalone mode, **when** `app/layout.tsx` is configured, **then** it exports `viewport` (Next.js `Viewport`) with `themeColor` as a **media-query array** — `{ media: "(prefers-color-scheme: light)", color: "#fafaf9" }` and `{ media: "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)", color: "#1c1917" }` (matching `--background` light/dark) — plus `width: "device-width"`, `initialScale: 1`, and **`viewportFit: "cover"`** (this is the missing piece that makes `env(safe-area-inset-*)` resolve to non-zero — see AC6), **and** `metadata.appleWebApp` enables iOS standalone (`capable: true`, a `statusBarStyle`, `title: "xynapse"`).

### Installability & launch (live-smoke — real browser)

5. **Given** the PWA is installable, **when** the user visits in Chrome (desktop/Android) or Safari (iOS), **then** the browser's native install prompt is available (Chrome/Android), iOS users can "Add to Home Screen" via the share sheet, **and** the installed PWA launches in **standalone** mode (no URL bar / browser nav).
6. **Given** the PWA launches from the home screen, **when** the app starts, **then** the cached app shell renders within ~0.3–0.5s (SW serves cached assets), the capture input is visible/functional before the Convex client connects (the Story 7.1 pre-hydration form), no splash screen blocks the user (the app shell **is** the splash), **and** the theme color matches the current light/dark mode (no jarring flash).
7. **Given** viewport and safe-area concerns, **when** the PWA renders on various devices, **then** `100dvh` (with `100vh` fallback) is used for full-height (already in `.app-shell`), **and** `env(safe-area-inset-top)`/`env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` are applied to the top status area and the tab bar / capture input area (`.top-bar`, `.tab-bar` exist; the capture input must respect the iOS home-indicator bottom inset), **all** now active because of `viewport-fit=cover` from AC4.
8. **Given** the Service Worker is updated, **when** a new app version is deployed, **then** the SW updates in the background and the new version activates on the next launch with **no disruptive "update available" modal** — already satisfied by `skipWaiting: true` + `clientsClaim: true` in `app/sw.ts:13-14`; verify and do not regress.

### Accessibility

9. **Given** PWA installation is evaluated for accessibility, **when** the installed app is inspected, **then** all prior-epic accessibility features (focus management, keyboard nav, screen-reader support, the Story 7.1 offline indicator and queued-card announcements) work **identically** in standalone mode without browser chrome — no feature may depend on browser UI.

stories.title: Responsive Design Polish
stories.acMarkdown:
### Responsive audit scope (the verification contract)

1. **Given** the responsive shell from Story 1.2 (mobile `<768px` via `useBreakpoint()` / Tailwind `md:`), **when** a comprehensive responsive audit is performed across all screens built in Epics 1–6, **then** every screen below is verified — by reading its component for layout-safe classes (deterministic) **and** by real-browser smoke at mobile (320px, 375px, 414px), tablet (768px, 1024px), and desktop (1280px, 1440px) widths (live-smoke; see Dev Notes → Verification boundary). The narrowest supported width is **320px**; nothing may overflow, clip, or horizontally scroll there.

### Capture tab

2. **Given** the capture thread on mobile at **320px** (smallest supported), **when** it renders, **then** the capture input, the send button (**48×48px** — `components/capture/CaptureInput.tsx:89` `h-12 w-12`, verify-only), and capture cards render with **no horizontal overflow or clipping**; cards keep their `p-4` padding (`ClassificationCard.tsx:299`) and the thread keeps its vertical spacing; **and** the fixed bottom tab bar does not overlap the input field (the composer clears the `.tab-bar` via `<main pb-16>` and the bar owns the bottom safe-area inset — Story 7.2 finding, verify-only).

### Browse tab

3. **Given** the Browse view on desktop (`≥768px`), **when** the category sidebar + notes list + detail panel render, **then** the multi-column layout has appropriate proportions with **no content overflow**, and the detail panel (`components/browse/NoteDetail.tsx`) shows full note detail without forcing scroll for typical note lengths. (Verify-only — layout is CSS-`md:`-driven.)

### Digest tab

4. **Given** the digest on mobile, **when** digest cards render, **then** they are scannable within the viewport with their established spacing and section headers visible without excessive scrolling; **and** the empty-state **"Go to Capture →" CTA meets a 44×44px minimum touch target** — **CODE CHANGE:** `EmptyDigestState.tsx` currently renders it at `h-9` (36px); raise it to ≥44px tall (e.g. add `min-h-11` to the CTA `className`, or an equivalent size) without breaking its centered layout.

### Settings

5. **Given** the settings pages on mobile (category management, audit trail, proactivity, trust), **when** they render, **then** all form controls are usable on small viewports with **no horizontal scrolling**; **and** the audit-trail table **stacks into a card-style layout on mobile** (no horizontal-scroll table). This is **already implemented** — `components/settings/AuditTrailBrowser.tsx:304-360` uses `block md:table-row` / `block md:table-cell` to collapse rows into stacked cards below `md`. Verify it still holds; do **not** rebuild it.

### Cross-breakpoint state preservation (UX-DR22)

6. **Given** the viewport transitions between mobile and desktop (e.g. tablet rotation, devtools resize), **when** the layout changes, **then** **scroll position, active tab, selected capture, and sidebar collapse state are preserved.** Verify the mechanism holds: active tab = route (`/capture`,`/browse`,`/digest`,`/settings`); selected capture = URL `?note=` param; sidebar collapse = `localStorage["xynapse-sidebar-collapsed"]` (`lib/hooks/useSidebarCollapse.ts`); scroll position is preserved **because the responsive layout toggles via CSS `md:` classes, not JS conditional unmount** — the scroll container (`<main className="overflow-auto">`) is never remounted on resize. **CRITICAL GUARD:** do not refactor any always-present scroll/selection container into a `useBreakpoint()` ternary that mounts/unmounts on resize — that would remount the subtree and lose scroll/state (see Dev Notes).

### PWA-specific responsive concerns

7. **Given** the PWA renders on various devices, **when** RTL text is captured (Arabic, Hebrew quotes or names), **then** `dir="auto"`-equivalent handling prevents layout breakage on textarea **and** card/detail text. **CODE CHANGE:** apply `data-user-content` to the six user-generated text elements in Dev Notes → RTL targets (the global rule `[data-user-content] { unicode-bidi: plaintext }` already exists in `app/globals.css:112-116`; the textarea is already covered). **And** portrait is the preferred mobile orientation with **no lock enforced** — landscape must still work (verify: no orientation lock anywhere; nothing to add).

### Quality gate (regression contract)

8. **Given** the existing suite is green (990 passed / 83 files at baseline), **when** this story's changes land, **then** `tsc --noEmit`, `eslint .`, and `vitest run` all pass with **0 regressions**, and `next build --webpack` still succeeds. New deterministic tests cover the two code changes (RTL attribute presence, CTA min-height class); the visual/overflow assertions are explicitly enumerated as live-smoke, **not** silently skipped.

stories.title: Accessibility Audit & CI Integration
stories.acMarkdown:
### axe-core in CI (NFR35)

1. **Given** the CI pipeline exists (`.github/workflows/ci.yml` from Story 1.1, currently a no-op echo at lines 21-25), **when** `@axe-core/playwright` is integrated, **then** the echo placeholder is replaced by a **real** `playwright test` invocation (browsers installed via `playwright install`), running **after** `vitest` (slower, acceptable post-vitest); **and** when the E2E environment is configured the suite runs on every push to `main` and **the CI build FAILS if any WCAG 2.1 AA violation is detected by axe-core**; **and** when the E2E secrets are absent the step **skips cleanly with a loud log line** (so the pre-secret solo-dev pipeline and fork PRs stay green) — activating the gate is the documented owner-action (Dev Notes → CI gate). The existing `tsc → eslint → vitest` order and the Convex deploy gate are preserved unchanged.

### Playwright E2E infrastructure

2. **Given** axe-core requires a browser context, **when** the Playwright test infrastructure is set up, **then** `@playwright/test` is added to `devDependencies` (pinned, latest stable — see Latest Tech), a `playwright.config.ts` is created (chromium project; `webServer` builds + serves the app; `baseURL` from env), and a minimal E2E suite is created in `e2e/` covering the core flows the axe scans run against: **onboarding, capture (+ correction), browse (+ note detail), digest, settings**; **and** each flow test navigates to the key screens and triggers an `AxeBuilder(...).withTags(['wcag2a','wcag2aa']).analyze()` scan **at stable states** (after content settles), asserting zero violations; **and** the tests authenticate via **`@clerk/testing/playwright`** (programmatic sign-in, no UI typing) and read **seeded** data (a `convex/seed.ts` test fixture), **never** production Convex; **and** `bun.lock` is updated so `bun install --frozen-lockfile` in CI resolves the new deps.

### Keyboard navigation audit (FR55, NFR30)

3. **Given** keyboard navigation is audited across the core flows, **when** a keyboard-only journey is verified, **then** these flows are completable without a mouse (enumerated, live-verified — see Dev Notes → keyboard journeys): Onboarding (Tab cards → Enter select → Tab Continue → Enter); Capture (Tab input → type → Enter send on desktop → Tab chip → Enter → arrows → Enter → Escape close); Browse (Tab filters → Enter → Tab cards → Enter open); Digest (Tab items → Enter expand → Tab nudge buttons); Settings (Tab controls → Enter/Space → Escape cancel); **and** focus is always visible (`--ring`); **and** focus returns to a logical position after overlay/dialog dismissal. Code-side: confirm `focus-visible:ring-ring` is present on interactive elements and that overlay dismissal restores focus (Radix default); the live tab-through is enumerated as owner live-verify.

### ARIA live regions audit (FR56)

4. **Given** ARIA live regions are audited, **when** dynamic content updates, **then** these announcements are verified to exist in code with the correct politeness (evidence in Dev Notes → ARIA audit map; **no rebuild** — these were built in Epics 2–5, this AC confirms them): capture submission `aria-busy`/"Understanding your thought"; classification arrival `aria-live="polite"` "Classified as [category], [confidence] confidence"; atomic split `aria-live="polite"`; correction `role="status"` "Category changed to [name]"; toasts `role="status"`; low-confidence `aria-describedby`. Any genuine missing/incorrect region found during the audit is fixed minimally and documented; the screen-reader *vocalization* itself is owner live-verify (Task 7 checklist).

### Visual accessibility audit (NFR28, NFR29, NFR31)

5. **Given** visual accessibility is audited, **then**: all text meets 4.5:1 (body) / 3:1 (large) contrast in both light and dark modes (NFR29) — **machine-verified by the real-browser axe `color-contrast` rule** (this is what jsdom never could; it closes the deferred AC8 color-contrast item — see Dev Notes); **and** `prefers-reduced-motion` disables the named animations — confirm `app/globals.css:173-183` covers `borderPulse`, `slideUp`/`slideDown`, and the skeleton pulse (verify-only; already present); **and** `prefers-contrast: more` is supported — confirm `app/globals.css:185-209` (verify-only; already present); **and** all text respects font-size up to 200% via `rem`/Tailwind text utilities with no clipping — confirm no hardcoded `px` font sizes block reflow (read-verify + live-smoke). Reduced-motion / 200%-reflow / cross-theme contrast as *rendered* are owner live-verify.

### Touch targets — broad NFR32 sweep (the code debt deferred from 7.3)

6. **Given** touch targets are audited on mobile, **when** all interactive elements are measured, **then** all tappable elements meet **44×44px minimum** (NFR32); **and** the send button meets 48×48px (already `h-12 w-12` — verify-only); **and** capture-thread category chips meet a 44px tap area. **CODE CHANGE:** sweep `components/**` for sub-44px tap targets and raise the genuine mobile ones to ≥44px using the **established convention** (`h-11 w-11` or `min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px]` — already used in `PushPrompt`, `MOCSuggestionCard`, `MOCDetail`, `CategorySuggestionCard`, `DigestCard`, `TabBar`). Concrete known gaps (Dev Notes → NFR32 targets): `ThemeToggleButton` (40px), `SearchTriggerButton` (40px), the `ClassificationCard` "Note actions" overflow trigger (~24px), `DesktopSidebar` toggle (40px, desktop). Deterministic class-presence tests cover each fix; the device-pixel measurement is owner live-verify.

### Manual screen-reader verification checklist

7. **Given** a manual screen-reader verification checklist is required, **when** it is authored, **then** a checklist file exists (`_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/a11y-screen-reader-checklist.md`) enumerating the core-flow checks for **VoiceOver (macOS/iOS)** and **NVDA (Windows)**, to be executed by the owner before public launch; **and** it notes the graph view is post-MVP and must provide a list-based alternative when built. This is a **document deliverable** (the actual SR pass is owner-action).

### Quality gate (regression contract)

8. **Given** the suite is green (**995 passed / 83 files** at baseline `c403a32`), **when** this story's changes land, **then** `tsc --noEmit`, `eslint .`, and `vitest run` all pass with **0 regressions**, and `next build --webpack` still succeeds. New deterministic vitest tests cover the NFR32 class fixes. The Playwright/axe suite is wired and **passes locally against a seeded dev deployment** (or is enumerated as owner live-verify if no local Clerk/Convex test env is available to the dev) — it is **never** marked "done" implying the authenticated axe run executed in CI when the gating secrets are not configured.

stories.title: Data Export

criteria.title: Export page renders with an "Export All Data" button + description
Tapping "Export All Data" runs `exportData` and produces the archive contents
Privacy compliance: no internal identifiers, no AI metadata, no audit data (NFR18)
Large exports: paginated batches + progress indicator + downloadable ZIP
Edge cases: special characters, slugified + de-duplicated filenames

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 7.5: Data Export` (lines 2154–2197). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**.

**AC1 — Export page renders with an "Export All Data" button + description**
- **Given** the user navigates to **Settings → Export**, **When** the export page renders, **Then** it displays an **"Export All Data"** button with the description: **"Download your entire knowledge base as Markdown files + JSON metadata"**.
- (New route `app/(app)/(main)/settings/export/page.tsx` (thin auth-gated client route, mirror `settings/categories/page.tsx`) → new presentational `components/settings/ExportPanel.tsx`. The Settings hub's **Export row must flip from the disabled "Coming soon" placeholder to an active `<Link href="/settings/export">`** — trap #1. Show the live note count beside the button when known (`getExportSummary`), e.g. "Download your entire knowledge base (N notes) as Markdown files + JSON metadata".)

**AC2 — Tapping "Export All Data" runs `exportData` and produces the archive contents**
- **Given** the user taps "Export All Data", **When** the **`exportData` public action** executes, **Then** it generates a downloadable archive containing:
  - **One Markdown file per note**: `notes/[note-title-slug].md` with the **capture text as content** and **YAML frontmatter** containing: **category name, confidence level, created date, correction count**.
  - A **`categories.json`** listing all categories with: **name, note count, creation date**.
  - A **`connections.json`** listing all connections with: **source note title, target note title, connection type, similarity score**.
  - A **`mocs.json`** listing all MOCs with: **title, note titles (not IDs), status, confidence**.
- (Architecture realisation — the **logical** archive above is assembled from a paginated server read + a client zip. `exportData` (public **action**) returns notes in **batches** plus, on the final page (`isDone`), the resolved relations (`categories`/`connections`/`mocs`); the **client** builds the Markdown + the three JSON files and zips them — trap #2/#6. The note "title" is **not a stored field** — derive it from `content` via a single pure `deriveNoteTitle` (server-side), so the same title is used both for the `.md` filename slug and for the title references inside `connections.json`/`mocs.json` — trap #4. "Confidence level" = the existing `confidenceTier` word (high/medium/low/…), `lib/confidence.ts` — reuse, don't reinvent — trap #3.)

**AC3 — Privacy compliance: no internal identifiers, no AI metadata, no audit data (NFR18)**
- **Given** the export is generated, **When** the content is reviewed for privacy compliance, **Then**:
  - **NO internal system identifiers** (Convex document IDs) appear anywhere in the export (NFR18).
  - **NO AI provider metadata** (model names, OpenRouter request IDs) appears in the export.
  - **NO audit trail data** is included (audit trail is system data, not user knowledge).
  - **Category names are used instead of category IDs** throughout.
- (Enforced **structurally**: no `v.id(...)` in any `convex/export.ts` return validator — trap #5. The export reads **only** `notes`/`categories`/`connections`/`mocs` — it **never reads `auditLog`** except the **bounded per-note correction COUNT** (a number derived from `auditLog`, not the audit rows themselves — `details.modelUsed`/`reasoning`/etc. never leave the server). `notes.embedding` (1536 floats) and all `_id`/`userId`/`categoryId`/`parentCaptureId` fields are projected **out**.)

**AC4 — Large exports: paginated batches + progress indicator + downloadable ZIP**
- **Given** the export file is large (**1000+ notes**), **When** the generation runs, **Then**:
  - the action **processes notes in paginated batches**;
  - a progress indicator shows: **"Exporting [X] of [N] notes..."**;
  - the final archive is provided as a **downloadable ZIP file**.
- (Required by Convex's 8 MiB / 8192-element limits as well as the AC — trap #6. The **client** loops `exportData({ cursor })` (`PAGE_SIZE` 100 — well under 8192), accumulating note records and updating an `aria-live="polite"` status `Exporting ${done} of ${N} notes…` after each page (`N` from `getExportSummary`). ZIP is built **client-side** with **`fflate`** (`zipSync` — pure-JS, ~8 kB, zero-dependency; **dynamic-import** it inside the click handler so it stays out of the initial bundle), wrapped in a `Blob`, and downloaded via a temporary `<a download="xynapse-export-YYYY-MM-DD.zip">` + `URL.createObjectURL` + `revokeObjectURL` — trap #7.)

**AC5 — Edge cases: special characters, slugified + de-duplicated filenames**
- **Given** the export is evaluated for edge cases, **When** notes with special characters in content are exported, **Then**:
  - **Markdown files handle special characters correctly** (no broken rendering);
  - **filenames are slugified** (no spaces, no special characters);
  - **duplicate slugs get a numeric suffix** (e.g., `my-idea.md`, `my-idea-2.md`).
- (Pure client helpers in `lib/exportArchive.ts`: `slugify` (lowercase, NFD-strip diacritics, non-alphanumeric runs → `-`, trim, length-cap, empty → `"note"`); `dedupeSlug` (global `Set`, append `-2`/`-3`… — trap #8); YAML frontmatter values are **safely quoted/escaped** so a category name containing `:` or `"` never breaks the block; the capture body is emitted verbatim under the frontmatter (Markdown body needs no escaping — fenced by the `---` delimiter above it). Title/slug truncation is by **code point** (`Array.from`, never split a surrogate pair — mirror `RelatedNotesPanel`'s `articleLabel`). All unit-tested.)

**AC6 — Accessibility**
- **Given** the export page is evaluated for accessibility, **When** the page is inspected, **Then**:
  - the export button has **`aria-label="Export all data as Markdown and JSON"`**;
  - the progress indicator uses **`aria-live="polite"`** for status updates;
  - the download link (when ready) is **keyboard-accessible and clearly labeled**.
- (The download fires programmatically (temporary anchor), so there is no persistent focusable "download link" to label — instead the **`aria-live` status announces completion** ("Exported N notes — download started") which satisfies the screen-reader intent; if a manual "Download again" affordance is added it must be a real keyboard-focusable `<button>`/`<a>` with a clear label. Each control keeps `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring`. An **axe** pass on the page (jsdom `color-contrast` rule disabled per project convention — deferred-work.md line 14).)

stories.title: Account & Data Deletion (Right to Erasure)

criteria.title: Settings → Account page: export reminder above a clearly-separated, non-inline Delete section
"Delete account" opens a guarded confirmation dialog (type-email-to-enable)
`deleteAllUserData` purges every collection, transactionally; sign-out + redirect + toast
Re-sign-in is a clean-slate new user (re-enters onboarding); nothing recoverable
Failure is handled: NFR41-safe log, user-facing retry message, rollback/flag
Erasure completeness: no dangling jobs/caches reference the deleted user

stories.acMarkdown:
> Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 7.6: Account & Data Deletion (Right to Erasure)` (lines 2201–2251). Transcribed faithfully; **(parenthetical notes are dev guidance, not new requirements)**.

**AC1 — Settings → Account page: export reminder above a clearly-separated, non-inline Delete section**
- **Given** the user navigates to **Settings → Account**, **When** the account settings page renders, **Then** it displays an **"Export your data first" reminder link** (to Story 7.5 export, `/settings/export`) directly **above** a **"Delete account"** section.
- **And** the "Delete account" section uses **`--destructive` color styling** but is **clearly separated from destructive-inline-action patterns** — **not a single primary button**; it **requires a multi-step confirmation**.
- (New route `app/(app)/(main)/settings/account/page.tsx` (thin `"use client"`, mirror `settings/export/page.tsx`) → presentational `components/settings/AccountSettings.tsx`. The Settings hub's **Account row must flip from the disabled "Coming soon" placeholder to an active `<Link href="/settings/account">`** — trap #4. "Multi-step" = the section's trigger button opens a confirmation **dialog** (step 1 → step 2); the dialog itself gates on a typed-email match (AC2). Use `variant="destructive"` / the `--destructive` token for the delete affordances; keep the section visually distinct (heading + impact copy), never a lone primary CTA.)

**AC2 — "Delete account" opens a guarded confirmation dialog (type-email-to-enable)**
- **Given** the user taps **"Delete account"**, **When** the confirmation dialog opens, **Then** it shows: **"This permanently deletes all your captures, categories, connections, MOCs, digests, and settings. This can't be undone. Have you exported your data?"**
