**AC3 — `deleteAllUserData` purges every collection, transactionally; sign-out + redirect + toast**
- **Given** the user confirms deletion, **When** the **`deleteAllUserData` public mutation** executes, **Then** all documents belonging to the user (filtered by `userId`) are deleted from: **`notes`, `categories`, `connections`, `mocs`, `digests`, `userSettings`, and `auditLog`** (and — per trap #1 — also `rateLimits`, `nudgeStates`, `categorySuggestions`, `reclassificationJobs`).
- **And** the deletion is **transactional** — either all collections are purged or none (no partial-state orphans).
- **And** the user's Clerk account is **signed out**; **optionally (configurable)** the Clerk user record is also deleted via Clerk Backend API `users.deleteUser(userId)`.
- **And** the user is **redirected to the sign-in page** with a brief toast: **"Your account and all data have been deleted."**
- (Single `authenticatedMutation` → atomic all-or-nothing for free (trap #2). **NFR14**: `ctx.userId` from the identity, **never an arg** (guidelines.md:182). Sign-out is **client-side** after the mutation resolves: `useClerk().signOut({ redirectUrl: "/sign-in" })`. Clerk-record deletion is the env-gated optional path (trap #3) — `process.env.CLERK_SECRET_KEY` present → schedule a V8 `internalAction` that `fetch`-DELETEs `https://api.clerk.com/v1/users/{userId}`; unset → no-op. The toast fires right before sign-out; its survival across the sign-out navigation is a **live-smoke** item (trap #7).)

**AC4 — Re-sign-in is a clean-slate new user (re-enters onboarding); nothing recoverable**
- **Given** the deletion has completed, **When** the user attempts to sign back in with the same OAuth identity, **Then** they are **treated as a new user** (re-enters onboarding via Story 1.3/1.4).
- **And** **no data from the previous account remains accessible or recoverable**.
- (Realized **structurally** by the purge — `userSettings` is gone, so `app/(app)/(main)/layout.tsx:38-42`'s `settings === null → router.push("/onboarding")` gate fires on the next authenticated load, re-entering the Story 1.3 category wizard / 1.4 proactivity onboarding. True even **without** Clerk-record deletion (the same `subject` returns, but with zero Convex data — trap #3). "Not recoverable" = the purge is a hard `ctx.db.delete`, no soft-delete/tombstone, no `auditLog` survivor (unlike single-note delete, which **keeps** audit rows — trap #5).)

**AC5 — Failure is handled: NFR41-safe log, user-facing retry message, rollback/flag**
- **Given** the deletion mutation **fails partway through**, **When** the failure is handled, **Then**:
  - the error is **logged via `console.error` without exposing user content** (NFR41);
  - the user sees: **"Couldn't complete deletion — try again or contact support"**;
  - any already-deleted collections are **rolled back if possible**, otherwise the partial state is **flagged for cleanup** (logged with `action: "deletion_failed"` in `auditLog` **IF `auditLog` itself hasn't been wiped**).
- (With the **atomic single-mutation** design, "rolled back if possible" is **always** the branch taken — Convex rolls the whole transaction back on any throw, so there is **never** a partial state and the "otherwise flag in auditLog" branch is **moot** (you also can't write a `deletion_failed` row inside a transaction that is itself rolling back — that write would roll back too). So: the **client** `try/catch` shows the retry toast; the **server** `console.error`s userId + a non-content error string before re-throwing. The `deletion_failed`-audit branch only becomes relevant if a future scale rewrite adopts the batched non-atomic pattern (trap #2) — note it, don't build it. **NFR41**: never log note `content`, category names, or PII — userId + action + error class only (mirror `classify.ts:645`).)

**AC6 — Accessibility of the deletion flow**
- **Given** the data deletion flow is evaluated for accessibility, **When** the dialog and input are inspected, **Then**:
  - the confirmation dialog uses **`role="alertdialog"`** with **`aria-describedby`** linking to the impact description;
  - **focus is trapped** within the dialog, **initial focus on the email confirmation input**;
  - **Escape dismisses** (equivalent to Cancel);
  - the **disabled→enabled state transition** on "Delete everything" is **announced via `aria-live="polite"`**: **"Delete button enabled"**;
  - all interactive elements have **visible focus indicators** and meet **4.5:1 contrast** minimum.
- (Radix `Dialog` (via `components/ui/dialog.tsx`) gives focus-trap + Escape-to-close natively (mirror `DeleteNoteDialog`). **Initial focus on the input**: Radix auto-focuses the first focusable child — force the input with `onOpenAutoFocus={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); inputRef.current?.focus(); }}` (the "Export first →" link would otherwise grab it). The **"Delete button enabled" announcement**: a `<span aria-live="polite" className="sr-only">` that renders that text only once the match flips false→true. `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring` on controls; **axe** pass on the panel + dialog (jsdom `color-contrast` rule disabled per project convention — `deferred-work.md`).)

**AC7 — Erasure completeness: no dangling jobs/caches reference the deleted user**
- **Given** the deletion satisfies GDPR right to erasure (NFR16), **When** the implementation is verified, **Then**:
  - **no background jobs, scheduled tasks, or derived caches** reference deleted user data after the mutation completes;
  - **any in-flight Convex actions scheduled via `scheduler.runAfter` for the deleted user are no-ops** (they **check for user/document existence before executing**).
- (The purge itself removes the derived caches (`rateLimits`, `nudgeStates`, `categorySuggestions`, `reclassificationJobs`, `mocs`, `digests`) — trap #1. For in-flight scheduled work, the codebase **already** follows the existence-gate pattern: every per-user scheduled function loads its target by id/userId first and no-ops when it's gone — `classifyNote`/`generateEmbedding` (`ctx.db.get(noteId)` → null guard), `reclassifyBatch` (loads the job, which the purge deletes → cancel/no-op), the three crons (`digest`/`cluster`/`categorySuggestions` query user data first, empty → skip). **Verify** each early-returns on a missing target; patch only if a gap is found — trap #6. No explicit job-cancellation API call is needed.)
